body.text: Auditory Shielding. Put on noise-canceling headphones or earplugs to shut out the environmental 'noise'.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the total amount of incoming data to allow the brain's 'switchboard' to cool down.

mind.text: The Foot-to-Floor Anchor. Focus entirely on the sensation of your feet inside your shoes and the pressure of the floor for one minute.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Moving focus from the high-speed 'head' energy to the stable, low-speed 'base' of the body.

social.text: The Question Embargo. Explicitly tell those around you: 'I am on a no-ask zone for the next hour. No questions unless it's an emergency.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Proactively stopping incoming social demands to prevent a high-arousal 'snap' or meltdown.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Twitchy
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel twitchy. You have an enormous amount of 'mobilization' energy ready to go, but nowhere to point it. You are like a racehorse behind the gate.
warning.blueprint: Unchanneled Sympathetic Energy. Your body has dumped adrenaline into your system to prepare for action, but since you aren't fighting or fleeing, the energy is vibrating in your muscles.
warning.physicalSensation: Restless legs; bouncing knees; tapping fingers; and a feeling of 'electricity' running under the skin.
warning.metaphor: A live wire that has fallen on the ground—sparking and jumping with energy that has no grounded destination.
warning.theImpulse: To pace the room, to bite your nails, to check your phone every 5 seconds, and to move without purpose.
warning.superpower: Ready-For-Action. You are primed. If a real emergency happened right now, you would be the first person to move.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'buzz' is making you feel anxious internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Entrainment Braking. Match your finger tapping to an external rhythm (like a clock or a steady beat), then gradually and consciously slow the tap down over 30 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'entrainment' to manually down-shift the nervous system by anchoring it to an external pace, pulling focus out of the internal jitter.

mind.text: Micro-Sorting. Find 10 small objects nearby (coins, pens, paperclips). Sort them by a specific rule: size, color, or weight. Focus entirely on the objects.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Channeling 'jittery' fine motor energy into an analytical external task to re-engage the prefrontal cortex and the environment.

social.text: The GIF Dispatch. Text a safe person a funny or high-energy GIF. Use the digital interaction to 'export' the jittery spark into a social connection.
social.physioTip: Best for: Converting unchanneled physical energy into a brief social 'ping,' which provides an external destination for the 'Live Wire' energy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too slow or annoying. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Burst Discharge. Perform 15 rapid squats or a quick set of push-ups against the wall. Give your large muscles a high-intensity job to do right now.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing a rapid metabolic 'sink' for excess adrenaline. Large muscle recruitment pulls the 'jitter' back into the skeletal core to be burned off.

mind.text: The Internal Metronome. Tap a steady beat on your own thigh. Count out loud: 1-2-3-4, matching the tap to your voice. Feel the touch on your skin.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using the prefrontal cortex to impose structure on chaotic motor energy, anchoring the brain back into the physical rhythm of the body.

social.text: The Percussive Hum. Hum a low, steady note with enough volume to feel your chest and throat vibrate. Focus entirely on the internal vibration.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using vocal cord vibration to stimulate the vagus nerve while providing an internal sensory anchor to 'ground' the twitchy electricity.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Surging
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel a surge. Something unexpected just happened, and your system has 'spiked' its energy to meet the new information. You are in a state of high-alert appraisal.
warning.blueprint: Acute Orientation Response. Your system is rapidly shifting gears to assess if a new stimulus is a threat or an opportunity, resulting in a sudden sympathetic surge.
warning.physicalSensation: A sudden 'gasp' or sharp inhale; wide eyes; a cold chill running down the spine; and a momentary 'freeze' followed by a racing heart.
warning.metaphor: A power surge in a building during a lightning storm—the lights flicker brightly for a second before the breakers reset.
warning.theImpulse: To gasp, to stare, to ask 'What was that?', and to feel a sudden prickle of cold or heat.
warning.superpower: Instant Adaptation. Your system just gave you a massive boost of awareness so you can survive the next 5 seconds, no matter what happens.
warning.generalGoal: Stabilizing the current. Slowing the return.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'spike' feels overwhelming. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Panoramic Scan. Slowly rotate your head to the far left and then slowly to the far right. Take in the full 180-degree view of the room's corners.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating cervical proprioceptors and the vestibular system to signal the brainstem that the external environment is currently stable.

mind.text: The Constant Hunt. Identify 3 large things in the room that have NOT changed in the last minute (e.g., the door, the floor, the heavy table).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a 'temporal anchor' to stabilize a brain that feels fragmented by a sudden change, grounding it in external continuity.

social.text: The Fact Check. Say out loud in a clear voice: 'That was a surprise, but the room is still here and I am safe.' Listen to your own voice.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'Thinking Brain' to override the survival brain's alarm, externalizing the logic to re-orient the prefrontal cortex.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels startling or sharp. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Resistance Exhale. Inhale for 3 seconds, then exhale through pursed lips (like blowing through a straw) for a long count of 8. Feel the pressure.
body.physioTip: Best for: Creating positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) to mechanically stimulate the vagus nerve and pull the heart rate back into the body's midline.

mind.text: The Solid Tether. Reach out and place your hand flat against a heavy, immovable object (a wall or desk). Squeeze slightly and feel the resistance in your hand.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'closed-chain' tactile input to pull a spaced-out brain back into the physical container of the arm and palm.

social.text: Diaphragm Anchor. Place one hand firmly on your belly and the other on your heart. Wait until you can feel the warmth of your hands on your skin.
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing a connection with the 'Self' after a shock has ejected focus to the periphery, providing a safe, internal embrace.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Burning
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are burning. Your system is radiating high-voltage energy because it's trying to incinerate a perceived threat or injustice. You aren't 'toxic'; you are protective.
warning.blueprint: Thermal sympathetic activation. Your metabolic rate has spiked to generate heat and power, preparing your body for a high-intensity defense.
warning.physicalSensation: Internal heat rising toward the face; dry mouth; a prickling sensation in the skin; and eyes that feel sharp or 'stinging'.
warning.metaphor: A furnace with the door wide open. The heat is useful for forging, but it consumes everything if it's not directed.
warning.theImpulse: To scorch others with words, to move fast and hard, and to find an immediate outlet for the internal fire.
warning.superpower: Pure Vitality. You have the energy of a star. When cooled slightly, this is the power of absolute conviction and boundary setting.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The fire is consuming you from within. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Radial Cooling. Run cold water over your inner wrists for 60 seconds. Focus entirely on the sensation of the cold liquid against your pulse points.
body.physioTip: Best for: Cooling the blood at high-circulation points where the radial artery is superficial, helping to lower systemic core temperature and signal safety.

mind.text: The Cold Search. Find one object in the room that is made of metal, stone, or glass. Look at it and imagine its temperature against your skin.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting the brain from 'hot' emotional triggers to cold environmental data, utilizing visual-thermal association to dampen arousal.

social.text: Thermal Alert. Text a safe person or say out loud: 'Running hot right now. Need a moment of cool silence.' Then move to a cooler physical space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using social agency to change your immediate thermal environment, forcing the body’s sensors to pivot toward homeostatic cooling.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment is adding fuel to your fire. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Sitali Cooling. Curl your tongue into a 'U' shape (or purse your lips) and inhale slowly through the mouth. Feel the cold air hitting the moisture on your tongue.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing evaporative cooling on the tongue to send an immediate 'temperature drop' signal to the brainstem through the glossopharyngeal nerve.

mind.text: The Blue Mist. Close your eyes. Imagine a cool, blue mist entering your lungs with every inhale, neutralizing the 'red' heat in your chest and belly.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using visceral imagery to facilitate systemic thermal settling, pulling the focus back to the internal process of regulation.

social.text: Internal Thermal Pivot. Visualize yourself stepping into a cold, quiet forest or a snow-covered field. Feel the silence and the cold on your face.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing a 'safe-place' mental image to simulate an environment that requires a lower metabolic output, pulling you back into a contained state.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Boiling
warning.validation: It makes sense that you are boiling. You are at the tipping point where liquid energy turns into steam pressure. Your system is ready to erupt to keep you from being suppressed.
warning.blueprint: High-pressure sympathetic surge. Your blood pressure and heart rate are at peak levels, creating a feeling of internal turbulence.
warning.physicalSensation: Churning in the stomach; a 'bubbling' feeling in the chest; and a vibrating quality to your limbs and jaw.
warning.metaphor: A pot on the stove with the lid rattling. The energy is contained, but only just barely.
warning.theImpulse: To boil over, to snap, to hiss, and to release the pressure through an explosive outburst.
warning.superpower: Transformative Force. Steam moves engines. This boiling energy is the precursor to massive change if the 'steam' is channeled through a pipe.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The pressure is shaking you from the inside. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Weighted Pace. Pace the room with heavy, rhythmic steps. As you walk, count the tiles or floorboards out loud: 1, 2, 3, 4.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic bone-loading and vocalization to anchor the brain in the external physical environment, pulling awareness away from the internal churn.

mind.text: Temporal Locking. Look at a clock with a second hand (or a digital timer). Match your finger-tapping to the steady 'tick' for 30 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Outsourcing your internal 'tempo' to a steady external source to manually override a racing, 'boiling' internal pulse.

social.text: The Core 'Why'. Say out loud to the room: 'I am furious because [X] is important to me.' Labeling the value makes the anger objective and external.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the prefrontal cortex to label the values behind the heat, which moves the energy from 'reactive boiling' to 'principled conviction'.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Everything feels like a trigger. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Vagal Toning (VOO). Inhale deeply and make a low, vibrating 'Vooooo' sound on the exhale. Focus entirely on the vibration in your chest and throat.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using internal sound vibration to stimulate the vagus nerve branch in the throat, providing a mechanical 'internal vent' for high pressure.

mind.text: The Steam Valve. Close your eyes. Imagine a safe pressure valve on top of your head slowly opening. Visualize the hot steam leaving your body through the valve.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using ideokinesis to facilitate a visceral release of the pressure, pulling the focus back to the internal sensation of cooling.

social.text: Impact Discharge. Press your hands together as hard as possible (the 'prayer' position) and feel the resistance of your own palms.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using a brief, high-intensity isometric impact to discharge motor energy within your own frame, re-establishing your physical boundaries.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Clenched
warning.validation: It makes sense that you are clenched. Your system has hardened your exterior to protect your vulnerability. You are a fortress under siege.
warning.blueprint: Protective Armoring. High sympathetic arousal is causing sustained muscular contraction (bracing) to protect your joints and organs from impact.
warning.physicalSensation: Tight jaw; shoulders up to the ears; fists balled; and a feeling of 'holding on' for dear life.
warning.metaphor: A fist that has forgotten how to open. Strong, but unable to feel or receive.
warning.theImpulse: To hold tighter, to refuse help, to grit your teeth, and to muscle through the pain.
warning.superpower: Unshakeable Strength. In this state, you are an anchor. Nothing can move you or break you while you are braced.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are a prisoner inside your own tension. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Pliability Scan. Identify three objects in your immediate view that are physically 'soft', 'malleable', or 'flexible' (e.g., a cushion, a plant, a curtain).
body.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain with external visual cues of 'non-rigidity' to counteract the internal 'armor' bias and pull awareness into the room.

mind.text: The Un-Armoring. Visualize yourself stepping out of a heavy, metal suit of armor in this exact room. See each piece land on the floor away from you.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using symbolic imagery to signal to the nervous system that the 'siege' is over and the environment is safe enough to soften.

social.text: Pressure Matching. If with a safe person, ask for a firm hand-squeeze. Match their pressure exactly for 5 seconds, then let go of the grip together.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using co-regulated resistance to satisfy the body's urge to 'hold' while tethering that effort to a safe external human anchor.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a physical threat. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Full-Body Flush (PIR). Squeeze every muscle in your body (fists, jaw, toes) even tighter for 5 seconds. On a long exhale, drop the tension all at once.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing Post-Isometric Relaxation (PIR) to bypass the brain's 'guarding' reflex, pulling awareness into the muscles to force a systemic release.

mind.text: The Knot Search. Close your eyes and locate the absolute 'center' of the clench in your body. Observe its shape and density from the inside.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'being' the tension to 'observing' the tension, which re-engages the prefrontal cortex with your internal state.

social.text: TMJ Release. Place your own fingertips on the hinges of your jaw. Apply gentle pressure while silently saying: 'It is safe to let the guard down.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Deactivating the primary cranial 'clench' trigger point, which helps pull the spaced-out system back into a regulated internal baseline.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Braced
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel braced. You are expecting an impact, so your system is pre-loading your muscles to absorb the blow.
warning.blueprint: Preparatory Sympathetic Load. Your brain is anticipating a challenge and has 'tightened the strings' of your system to ensure stability.
warning.physicalSensation: Lower back tension; knees locked; holding the breath; and a feeling of 'readiness' that is starting to ache.
warning.metaphor: A bridge under a heavy load—straining against the weight, but holding firm.
warning.theImpulse: To stay in one position, to monitor every sound, and to prepare for the worst.
warning.superpower: Durability. You are built to endure. Your system is currently maximizing its defensive architecture to keep you upright.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your internal structure is under strain. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Horizontal Scan. Identify three long, horizontal lines in the room (a shelf, a floorboard, a window frame). Follow each one slowly from end to end.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'visual horizons' to stabilize spatial orientation, which signals the brain to lower the high-arousal 'guarding' response.

mind.text: The Safe Container. Imagine yourself inside a solid, transparent dome. It is an impenetrable barrier that keeps all external pressure away from your skin.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a symbolic visual boundary to reduce the 'startle' reflex and the neurological need for constant muscular armor.

social.text: The Safety Ping. Text a trusted person: 'In bracing mode. Just sending a ping to stay connected.' Observe the phone as a physical link to the outside world.
social.physioTip: Best for: Outsourcing the search for safety to a reliable social tether, which pulls focus out of the internal bracing and toward the environment.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels unpredictable. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Spinal Unload. Place your hands on a table or chair. Push down firmly to let your spine grow tall, then slowly soften your elbows and feel your weight settle.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'closed-chain' resistance to re-align the vertebrae and then testing the system's ability to yield as the 'bracing' is consciously removed.

mind.text: The Lateral Shift. Slowly shift your weight from your left foot to your right. Focus entirely on the changing pressure in your heels and the soles of your feet.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Unlocking the knees and engaging the vestibular system to pull awareness back into the vertical axis and the support of the ground.

social.text: Wall Surrender. Lean your back fully against a solid wall. Feel the contact points from your sacrum to your shoulder blades. Let the wall 'carry' your spine.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing large-surface tactile contact to provide the nervous system with undeniable proof of external support, allowing the core to inhabit the body.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Racing
warning.validation: It makes sense that everything feels like it's racing. Your system just had a massive intake of information and is trying to process it at 100mph to catch up.
warning.blueprint: Tachy-psychia (Time Distortion). High adrenaline is making your brain process the environment faster than normal, making the world seem like it's in fast-forward.
warning.physicalSensation: Fluttering in the chest; eyes darting; thoughts jumping from topic to topic; and cold sweat.
warning.metaphor: A film being played at double speed—you can see everything, but you can't feel the meaning of anything.
warning.theImpulse: To talk fast, to move erratically, and to 'do everything' at once to stop the racing feeling.
warning.superpower: Rapid Integration. You are a high-speed processor. You can see connections and details that others miss because your 'shutter speed' is so high.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your internal thoughts are a blur. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Honey-Motion Orienting. Rotate your head to look around the room as if you were moving through thick honey. Move as slowly as possible, noticing every detail.
body.physioTip: Best for: Manually slowing the vestibular-ocular reflex to 'reset' the brain's frame-rate and reduce the internal sense of racing.

mind.text: High-Definition Description. Pick one external object. Describe its color and texture to yourself in extreme detail (e.g., 'A matte, dusty navy with a slight metallic sheen').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to switch from high-speed 'threat scanning' to slow 'analytical inspection,' pulling awareness into the room.

social.text: Slow-Talk Request. If with a safe person, ask them: 'Could you tell me a story or a recipe very slowly?' Focus entirely on the gaps between their words.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'social entrainment'—hitching your internal speed to someone else's slower vocal rhythm to pull you out of the mental blur.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world looks like a blur or a movie. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Parasympathetic Brake (4-7-8). Inhale through the nose for 4, hold for 7, and exhale through pursed lips for a slow count of 8. Feel your ribs move.
body.physioTip: Best for: Mechanically stimulating the vagus nerve to lower the heart rate, forcing the brain to re-occupy the biological midline.

mind.text: The Pulse Sync. Find your pulse at your wrist or neck. Count it silently for 30 seconds. Focus on the physical 'thump' against your finger.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a concrete, biological 'metronome' to anchor a racing mind back into the physical reality of the heart.

social.text: Thermal Grounding. Place your palms flat on a cool, solid surface (like a table or tile). Squeeze slightly and feel the cold against your skin.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using constant thermal data to 'ground' excess sympathetic energy, pulling awareness back into the hands and core.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Volatile
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel volatile. Your system is highly reactive because it detects a lack of safety. You are like a sensitive alarm system currently set to its highest trigger level.
warning.blueprint: Sympathetic Hyper-reactivity. Your nervous system is primed for a 'snap' response. The brain is scanning for any further threat to justify an immediate explosion.
warning.physicalSensation: Hot, shallow breathing; a tight, restless feeling in the chest; and eyes that feel like they are darting or scanning the room rapidly.
warning.metaphor: Dry brush in a drought. One small spark could start a fire, so your system is bracing for the flame.
warning.theImpulse: To react sharply to small annoyances, to pace, and to use 'biting' words to create distance.
warning.superpower: Rapid Response. You are incredibly quick right now. If directed toward a task, you could complete it in record time.
warning.generalGoal: Lowering the sensitivity. Creating a buffer.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel like you're 'fuming' internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Sensory Pivot. Physically leave the room you are in. Move to a space with different lighting, a different temperature, or step outside.
body.physioTip: Best for: Forcing a total reset of the 'orienting response' by changing the entire data feed, pulling awareness out of the internal 'fume' loop.

mind.text: The 3-3-3 Grounding. Name 3 things you see in the room and 3 distinct sounds you hear. Then move 3 small, separate parts of your body (e.g., big toe, pinky, tongue).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to cycle through multiple external sensory channels, which disrupts the hyper-fixated 'threat scan' typical of volatility.

social.text: The Extinguisher Signal. Text a safe person a 'fire extinguisher' emoji 🧯. If alone, say out loud to the room: 'I am running hot, I need this space to be quiet.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a social boundary with zero verbal tax, externalizing the need for a 'buffer' before an explosive interaction occurs.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels irritating and abrasive. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Vagal Splash. Splash cool water on your face, focusing on the area around your eyes, or hold a cold compress to the back of your neck. Focus on the temperature shock.
body.physioTip: Best for: Triggering the 'Mammalian Dive Reflex' via the trigeminal nerve to rapidly lower heart rate and pull the system back into a regulated internal baseline.

mind.text: The Resistance Exhale. Inhale through your nose for 3 seconds, then exhale through very small, pursed lips for 6 seconds. Feel the pressure in your chest.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using positive expiratory pressure to stimulate the vagus nerve and mechanically override the sympathetic surge from within the body.

social.text: Label the Spark. Identify the specific thing currently irritating you. Say silently: '[X] is the spark, but I am the one choosing to watch it burn.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Creating 'cognitive distance' by labeling the trigger, which shifts the brain from the reactive amygdala to the analytical prefrontal cortex.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Trembling
warning.validation: It makes sense that you are trembling. This is your body's natural way of trying to 'shake off' or discharge trapped survival energy. It’s a sign that your system is trying to return to balance.
warning.blueprint: Neurogenic Tremoring. Your sympathetic system is attempting to release the high-voltage charge of a 'fright' response. It is a biological 'reset' button.
warning.physicalSensation: Involuntary shivering or shaking in the hands, legs, or jaw; chattering teeth; and a 'fluttering' sensation in the stomach.
warning.metaphor: A small bird ruffling its feathers after a close call with a predator—shaking to let the fear fall away.
warning.theImpulse: To hide the shaking, to tighten your muscles to stop the movement, or to feel 'out of control'.
warning.superpower: The Great Release. Your body is doing the hard work for you. Shaking is the most direct path back to the Green Zone.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The shaking feels scary from the inside. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Supported Discharge. Lean your back or hands firmly against a solid wall. Allow the shaking to continue while focusing on the cold, unmoving support of the bricks or wood.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing an external 'immovable' reference point so the brain feels safe enough to allow the neurogenic tremor to complete its discharge cycle without panic.

mind.text: The Calm Witness. Visualize a safe person or pet sitting in the room with you, watching the shaking with steady, kind, and non-judgmental eyes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from internal 'alarm' to an external 'witness' perspective, reducing the shame-response that often blocks the discharge.

social.text: The Steady Anchor. If someone safe is nearby, ask them to place a steady, unmoving hand firmly on your shoulder. Focus on the stillness of their hand.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using an external human anchor to provide a 'still' point of reference, helping the brain distinguish between the shaking self and the stable world.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel detached or 'spaced out' from the trembling. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Somatic Containment. Wrap yourself tightly in a blanket or give yourself a firm 'self-hug' (hands crossing to opposite shoulders). Squeeze until you feel your ribs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using deep pressure (DTP) to define the body's 'envelope,' helping the brain re-tether to the physical midline during the tremor.

mind.text: Tremor Tracking. Close your eyes. Observe the rhythm of the tremors for 10 seconds. Is it fast or slow? Locate the 'epicenter'—is it in your jaw, chest, or legs?
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from a state of 'being shaken' to a state of 'observing movement,' which re-engages the prefrontal cortex with the internal body.

social.text: Frequency Matching (VOO). Inhale and make a low, vibrating 'Vooooo' sound on the exhale. Try to match the internal vibration of your voice to the tremor in your body.
social.physioTip: Best for: Harmonizing the 'fright' vibration with an intentional vocal vibration, signaling the brain that the internal movement is organized and intentional.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Pressurized
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel pressurized. You are holding a lot of 'mobilization' fuel inside, but the 'outlets' feel blocked. You are a container of high-potential energy.
warning.blueprint: Inhibited Sympathetic Drive. Adrenaline is high, but your 'social engagement' system is trying to keep a lid on it, creating an internal pressure cooker effect.
warning.physicalSensation: Pressure behind the eyes; a tight jaw; and a feeling like your skin is 'too tight' for your body.
warning.metaphor: A garden hose with a kink in it. The water is trying to flow, but the pressure is building up at the blockage.
warning.theImpulse: To snap, to grit your teeth, and to find something—anything—to push against.
warning.superpower: Internal Drive. This pressure is the 'fuel' of change. If you can point it at a task, you will be unstoppable.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The pressure feels like it's building in your head. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Horizon Scan. Look as far away as possible—out a window or down a long hallway. Follow the furthest horizontal line you can see with your eyes.
body.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'threat-tunnel vision' by expanding the visual field, which signals the superior colliculus to lower the sympathetic 'pressure' signal.

mind.text: The 50% Load. Place your hands flat on a wall or heavy desk. Push with 50% of your strength. Imagine the 'steam' moving from your chest, through your arms, and into the wood/stone.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a sub-maximal 'vent' for motor energy. The external resistance proves you can move the load, which reclaim agency from the 'inside' pressure.

social.text: The Boundary Declaration. State out loud to the room: 'I am running high on energy right now. I need a 5-minute buffer.' Then physically take up more space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Exercising social agency to expand your 'personal space' perimeter, which tells the brain it is no longer being 'squeezed' by external demands.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like it's 'pressing' on you. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Jaw Release. Open your mouth wide in a silent yawn. Use your fingertips to gently massage the hinges of your jaw. Let the tongue drop away from the roof of your mouth.
body.physioTip: Best for: Deactivating the 'Masseter-Temporal' clench. As the primary cranial bracing point, releasing here tells the brain it's safe to inhabit the 'midline' again.

mind.text: The Balloon Nozzle. Close your eyes. Imagine the pressure is a balloon. With every exhale, visualize a steady, narrow stream of air escaping from a nozzle in your chest.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using visceral imagery to facilitate a slow, controlled internal discharge of sympathetic energy, preventing a sudden reactive 'snap'.

social.text: The Vocal Hiss. Take a deep inhale through the nose. Exhale through your teeth with a long, steady 'Ssssss' sound. Focus entirely on the vibration in your teeth.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a mechanical and auditory 'vent' that mimics a physical pressure valve. It pulls the spaced-out brain back into the internal rhythm of the breath.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Vibrating
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are vibrating. Your system is 'idling' at a high RPM, ready to move but waiting for a clear direction.
warning.blueprint: Preparatory Sympathetic Arousal. Your motor neurons are firing at a high frequency to prepare your muscles for action, even if you are standing still.
warning.physicalSensation: A 'humming' or 'buzzing' feeling in the hands and feet; a butterfly sensation in the stomach; and an inability to sit still.
warning.metaphor: A beehive in the summer—constant, high-frequency movement that sounds like a single, steady note.
warning.theImpulse: To fidget, to check your phone repeatedly, and to talk faster than usual.
warning.superpower: Alert Readiness. You are highly 'online'. Your reaction time is currently at its peak.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'hum' feels anxious internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: External Entrainment. Find a steady physical rhythm in the room (a ticking clock, a humming fan, or a bird chirping). Tap your finger to that external beat.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'motor entrainment' to pull awareness out of the internal 'vibration' and tether the nervous system to a predictable external pace.

mind.text: Texture Labeling. Pick one object in the room. Describe its texture in your head using 5 specific sensory words (e.g., 'gritty', 'cool', 'slick', 'woven', 'dense').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the associative cortex to shift the brain from high-speed 'threat scanning' to slow-speed 'evaluation,' pulling focus into the room.

social.text: The Buzz Ping. Text a safe friend a 'bee' emoji 🐝. If alone, say out loud: 'I am buzzing right now, I need to ground.' Listen to the sound of your voice.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing the internal state to reduce the 'social masking' tax. Labeling the 'buzz' to the environment tells the brain the secret is out and it can settle.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels a bit blurry or too fast. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Butterfly Hug. Cross your arms over your chest. Slowly tap your shoulders, alternating left and right, like a slow metronome. Feel the touch on your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using bilateral alternating stimulation to pull a 'spaced-out' brain back into its midline and organize sympathetic 'noise' into a coherent signal.

mind.text: The Internal Hum. Hum a steady, low note. Focus entirely on the physical vibration in your throat and chest. Feel the 'hum' moving your own ribs.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the laryngeal branch of the vagus nerve to provide an internal 'anchor' that matches and then settles the vibrating sensation.

social.text: Prosody Focus. If someone is talking, ignore their words. Focus entirely on the melodic 'rise and fall' of their voice. Feel it as a wave of sound.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing vocal prosody to signal the ventral vagal system that the environment is safe, pulling the 'flickering' focus back into a state of presence.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Fidgety
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel fidgety. You have a surplus of 'mobilization' energy and your system is trying to find a productive way to use it.
warning.blueprint: Low-level Sympathetic Discharge. Your body is using 'micro-movements' (fidgeting) to bleed off excess adrenaline and keep the system from becoming overloaded.
warning.physicalSensation: Bouncing legs; tapping fingers; playing with hair or jewelry; and a constant need to shift your posture.
warning.metaphor: A puppy waiting by the door with a leash in its mouth. Ready to go, eager, and full of undirected movement.
warning.theImpulse: To start three different tasks at once, to clean something, or to pace while thinking.
warning.superpower: Active Creativity. This restlessness is the 'itch' that leads to discovery and getting things done.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'itch' to move. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Resistance Game. If with someone, engage in a quick thumb-war or a brief 'palm-push' match. If alone, press your palms together firmly for 10 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast physical resistance to provide an immediate external anchor, discharging restless energy through interaction with the environment.

mind.text: The 2-Minute Triage. Identify 3 tiny physical tasks you can see in the room (e.g., move a glass, straighten a pillow, close a drawer). Do ONE right now.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Channeling restless motor energy into a 'completion loop,' which provides an external dopamine hit and anchors the brain in the room.

social.text: The Micro-Service. Complete one tiny physical task that benefits the space or someone else (e.g., tidying a shared surface or refilling a water bottle).
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'tend-and-befriend' circuit to shift away from internal jittery arousal toward external social regulation.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Everything feels like a distraction. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Extremity Shake. Shake your hands and feet vigorously for 10 seconds. Stop abruptly and focus entirely on the 'tingle' of the blood returning to your fingers.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging sympathetic build-up and then immediately pulling focus back to the skin's surface and muscle spindles to re-occupy the body.

mind.text: The Texture Map. Close your eyes. Focus on the sensation of your clothes against your skin. Move slightly and identify if the texture is rough, smooth, or heavy.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from outward-focused distraction to inward-focused tactile grounding, pulling the 'spaced-out' brain back into the somatic envelope.

social.text: Object Manipulation. Find a 'fidget' object—a pen to click, a stone to rub, or a piece of fabric. Focus entirely on the internal resistance of the object against your fingers.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a continuous sensory-motor loop that occupies the 'excess' motor drive, anchoring the self back to the hands.

warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Pounding
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel a pounding sensation. Your heart is increasing its tempo to prepare your muscles for action. Even if you aren't moving, your body is ready to protect you.
warning.blueprint: Cardiovascular Activation. Your sympathetic nervous system has increased your heart rate and stroke volume, making the pulse feel more prominent in your chest, neck, or ears.
warning.physicalSensation: A rhythmic thumping in the chest; a pulsing feeling in the temples or throat; and a sense of internal 'drums' beating faster than the environment.
warning.metaphor: An engine idling at a high speed, ready for the race to start. The vibration is felt through the whole frame of the car.
warning.theImpulse: To check your pulse, to breathe rapidly, and to look for an immediate way to 'quiet' the noise in your chest.
warning.superpower: Circulatory Power. Your body is efficiently moving oxygen and fuel exactly where it needs to go. You are physically primed for high performance.
warning.generalGoal: Steadying the rhythm. Externalizing the beat.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'thump' is making you feel more anxious. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Square Tracing. Find 5 rectangular objects in the room (a window, a book, a screen). Slowly trace their four corners with your eyes, counting 1-2-3-4 for each.
body.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the oculomotor muscles in a structured geometric pattern to 'mark' the external environment, pulling focus away from internal cardiac noise.

mind.text: Serial 7s. Count backward from 100 by subtracting 7 each time (100, 93, 86...). Focus entirely on the math and say the numbers out loud.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to exit a 'limbic hijack' by engaging the prefrontal cortex in a high-load analytical task, overriding internal panic signals.

social.text: Prosody Anchor. If someone is speaking, ignore their words. Focus entirely on the melodic 'rise and fall' of their voice. Use it to tether yourself to the room.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing external co-regulation to synchronize your internal tempo with a stable social signal, anchoring you in the shared space.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels too loud or fast for your heartbeat. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Baroreceptor Reset. Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds, then exhale through pursed lips (like a straw) for a slow 8 seconds. Feel the pressure change in your chest.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using PEEP (Positive End-Expiratory Pressure) to stimulate the baroreceptors and the vagus nerve, mechanically forcing the heart rate to drop from within.

mind.text: Rhythmic Entrainment. Tap a slow, steady beat on your own thigh. Focus on the physical contact of your hand against your leg. Make the tap the dominant rhythm.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'motor-pacing' to provide the brain with a competing, intentional tactile rhythm that overrides the involuntary pounding of the pulse.

social.text: The Heart Acknowledgment. Place your own hand firmly over your heart. Say silently: 'I hear you, heart. Thank you for the energy. We are inside, we are safe.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'self-affiliation' and tactile containment to reduce the fear-response to internal sensations, pulling awareness back to the somatic midline.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Guarded
warning.validation: It makes sense that you feel guarded. Your system is protecting your softest parts because it's not yet sure if the current environment is fully safe.
warning.blueprint: Defensive Armoring. A state of mild sympathetic arousal where the muscles of the core, jaw, and shoulders slightly contract to protect vital areas from potential impact.
warning.physicalSensation: Shoulders slightly raised; jaw held tight; and a subtle 'bracing' in the belly. You feel like you are wearing an invisible suit of armor.
warning.metaphor: A drawbridge that has been raised halfway. You aren't closed off, but you are carefully controlling who and what gets in.
warning.theImpulse: To cross your arms, to weigh your words carefully, and to monitor the movements of others in the room.
warning.superpower: Strategic Discernment. You are not being 'cold'; you are being wise. You are curating your vulnerability until safety is proven.
warning.generalGoal: Testing the perimeter. Softening the edges.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel tight and 'walled in' internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The 180-Degree Scan. Slowly turn your head to the far left and far right. Notice 3 neutral objects (e.g., a door handle, a chair leg) that are definitively unmoving.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using cervical rotation to signal the superior colliculus that the periphery is clear, allowing the brain to release 'braced' postural muscles.

mind.text: Detail Appreciation. Pick one interesting object in the room. Silently list 3 precise details (e.g., 'the grain of the wood', 'the way light hits the edge').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'broad threat-scanning' to 'narrow detailed observation,' which forces the amygdala to yield to the prefrontal cortex.

social.text: The Half-Smile (Vagal Ping). Look at a neutral object and give it a tiny, almost invisible 'half-smile'. Notice the slight release in your jaw and around your eyes.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the facial feedback loop to trigger the ventral vagus, signaling the brain that the environment is safe enough for social engagement.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like it's watching you. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Lateral Costal Breathing. Place your hands on the sides of your ribcage. Inhale and try to push your hands out to the sides with your ribs. Feel the expansion.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rib expansion to create internal space without the 'vulnerability' of a full belly breath, softening the intercostal tension from the inside out.

mind.text: The Safe Haven. Close your eyes for 5 seconds. Imagine a specific place where you feel 100% safe. Visualize the colors, the ground beneath you, and the temperature.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using mental architecture to provide the 'safety data' that the current environment is failing to deliver, pulling awareness back to a centered self.

social.text: The Protective Rub. Gently rub your own upper arms or shoulders with your palms. Focus entirely on the warmth and the texture of your own skin.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing 'self-soothing' tactile input to lower the guarding reflex in the trapezius, re-establishing your body as a safe place to be.

warning.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

warning.title: Scanning
warning.validation: It makes sense that you are scanning. Your brain is trying to predict and prevent problems by looking for any small cue of trouble. Your 'radar' is just working overtime.
warning.blueprint: Hyper-Vigilant Orienting. The nervous system has narrowed your visual and auditory focus to detect threats, often leading to 'tunnel vision' or an inability to rest the eyes.
warning.physicalSensation: Eyes darting from object to object; a feeling of 'lightness' in the head; and a neck that feels slightly stiff from constant movement.
warning.metaphor: A lighthouse beam swinging rapidly across the water, searching for a rock that might not even be there.
warning.theImpulse: To check your phone, to look at the door, and to mentally rehearse 'what-if' scenarios over and over.
warning.superpower: High-Speed Pattern Recognition. You are currently a genius at noticing details. Your system is protecting your future by analyzing the present.
warning.generalGoal: Grounding the gaze. Broadening the view.
warning.Bridge of Understanding: This is your 'Vibe.' From deep discomfort or pain (-6) to pure joy and ease (+5). It’s the 'Quality' of your current experience.

Trauma-Informed.title: Surging
Trauma-Informed.validation: It makes sense that you feel a surge. Something unexpected just happened, and your system has 'spiked' its energy to meet the new information. You are in a state of high-alert appraisal.
Trauma-Informed.blueprint: Acute Orientation Response. Your system is rapidly shifting gears to assess if a new stimulus is a threat or an opportunity, resulting in a sudden sympathetic surge.
Trauma-Informed.physicalSensation: A sudden 'gasp' or sharp inhale; wide eyes; a cold chill running down the spine; and a momentary 'freeze' followed by a racing heart.
Trauma-Informed.metaphor: A power surge in a building during a lightning storm—the lights flicker brightly for a second before the breakers reset.
Trauma-Informed.theImpulse: To gasp, to stare, to ask 'What was that?', and to feel a sudden prickle of cold or heat.
Trauma-Informed.superpower: Instant Adaptation. Your system just gave you a massive boost of awareness so you can survive the next 5 seconds, no matter what happens.
Trauma-Informed.generalGoal: Stabilizing the current. Slowing the return.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'spike' feels overwhelming. Goal: Slow Exhale.
pathA.body: The Long Exhale. Inhale for 3 seconds, then exhale through pursed lips for a count of 8. Repeat 3 times.
pathA.mind: Name the Fact. Say out loud: 'That was a surprise, and I am safe right now.'
pathA.social: The Hand-on-Belly. Place your hand on your stomach and wait until you can feel it move with your breath again.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels startling or sharp. Goal: Re-orienting to Safety.
pathB.body: Look slowly to the left and slowly to the right. Take in the full 180° view of your surroundings.
pathB.mind: Identify 3 things in the room that have NOT changed in the last minute.
pathB.social: Reach out and touch a solid object (a desk, a wall). Feel its coldness and stability.

Trauma-Informed.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Immobilized
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel immobilized. Your system prioritizes survival-stillness over action. You're not stuck forever; this is a pause button for safety.
pathB.blueprint: Dorsal vagal dominance. Your parasympathetic system has taken full control, dropping your heart rate into a state of metabolic rest.
pathB.physicalSensation: A profound, leaden heaviness. Movement feels like a mechanical impossibility.
pathB.metaphor: A vehicle stalled on the tracks; the engine is cold, and the key won't turn, waiting for a jumpstart.
pathB.theImpulse: To surrender, sink into the floor, and become entirely invisible.
pathB.superpower: The Emergency Brake. Your body is numbing you to save you from unbearable distress, buying time until safety returns.

body.text: The Weighted Blanket. Use a heavy coat or blanket to feel the pressure on your legs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing deep pressure input to soothe a vulnerable nervous system.

mind.text: Word Repetition. Whisper a single comforting word (e.g., 'Soft', 'Safe') five times.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using a repetitive vocal 'mantra' to calm the chatter of a depleted brain.

social.text: Passive Connection. Listen to a familiar podcast or audiobook just to hear a human voice.
social.physioTip: Best for: Receiving the safety of human prosody (voice tone) without the demand of interaction.

mind.title: Hibernating
mind.validation: It makes sense that you feel like hibernating. Your tank is empty and your body has hit the emergency brake. You are safe to pause and thaw at your own pace.
mind.blueprint: Metabolic conservation mode. Your heart rate and breathing drop to preserve core vitality.
mind.physicalSensation: A profound, immovable heaviness; a drop in body temperature; the feeling of an empty chest cavity.
mind.metaphor: A machine cutting power to all non-essential systems to save the last drop of battery.
mind.theImpulse: To isolate, lie down, and retreat into a dark, quiet sanctuary.
mind.superpower: Biological Shielding. Your system is masterfully powering down to protect you from overwhelming stress.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and trapped in an internal void. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Sound Waves. Play activating music and let the vibrations fill the room.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using external vibrations to jumpstart a system that feels 'unplugged'.

mind.text: External Focus. Name 3 objects in the room and whisper their shapes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from internal rumination to objective exteroception.

social.text: Digital Ping. Text a safe person a single emoji to acknowledge the outside world.
social.physioTip: Best for: Creating a low-energy bridge back to co-regulation without overwhelm.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are spaced out and disconnected from the room. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Sensory Shock. Splash cold water on your face or feel the floor supporting your weight.
body.physioTip: Best for: A thermal or tactile 'reset' to bring the nervous system back into the present.

mind.text: Boundary Tracing. Mentally state: 'My feet end at the floor. My back ends at the chair.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Establishing the physical container of the body when feeling 'thin' or 'faded'.

social.text: Internal Anchor. Place a hand over your heart and recall the feeling of a safe hug.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using somatic memory to activate self-soothing when alone.

mind.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathB.title: Immobilized
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel immobilized. Your system prioritizes survival-stillness over action. You're not stuck forever; this is a pause button for safety.
pathB.blueprint: Dorsal vagal dominance. Your parasympathetic system has taken full control, dropping your heart rate into a state of metabolic rest.
pathB.physicalSensation: A profound, leaden heaviness. Movement feels like a mechanical impossibility.
pathB.metaphor: A vehicle stalled on the tracks; the engine is cold, and the key won't turn, waiting for a jumpstart.
pathB.theImpulse: To surrender, sink into the floor, and become entirely invisible.
pathB.superpower: The Emergency Brake. Your body is numbing you to save you from unbearable distress, buying time until safety returns.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy, numb, and trapped inside an internal void. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Auditory Spark. Put on an activating piece of music and let the sound fill the room.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic vibrations to penetrate leaden heaviness and signal the brain that the environment is safe and moving.

mind.text: Shape Naming. Name 3 objects in the room and whisper what shape they are.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Gently re-engaging exteroception (external focus) to interrupt the internal void.

social.text: Text a safe person a single emoji just to ping the outside world.
social.physioTip: Best for: A low-stakes 'ping' of connection that requires zero verbal energy but confirms you aren't invisible.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are spaced out and disconnected from the room. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Temperature Shock. Splash cold water on your face, or feel the solid surface supporting your weight.
body.physioTip: Best for: Forcing a 'bottom-up' sensory reset through the thermal or tactile receptors in the skin.

mind.text: Physical Boundaries. Mentally state: 'My feet end at the floor. My back ends at the chair.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Defining the physical container of the body when the sense of 'self' feels faded or thin.

social.text: Internal Anchor. Place a hand over your heart and imagine a safe hug, or focus on a pet nearby.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing non-verbal safety through somatic memory or the presence of a co-regulating person/pet.

pathB.text: Temperature Shock. Splash cold water on your face, or hold a cold object (like a beverage) against your wrist.

text.title: Hollow
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel hollow. Depletion signals retreat. You are not broken; this void is a protocol protecting you from system failure.
text.blueprint: Energy-conserving stasis. Your nervous system has ceased non-essential functions to conserve your last drops of fuel.
text.physicalSensation: The physical feeling of an empty chest cavity; a lack of internal substance or life force.
text.metaphor: A dried-up riverbed or a winter landscape where everything has gone dormant to survive the cold.
text.theImpulse: To fade away, become invisible, and detach from an environment that feels barren.
text.superpower: Total Conservation. Your system masterfully strips away all excess to preserve core vitality for eventual renewal.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel hollowed-out, an internal desert of depletion. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Friction Heat. Rub your palms against each other and feel the heat generate between them.
body.physioTip: Best for: Generating internal warmth and proprioceptive input to remind the system it is still physically present.

mind.text: Color Anchor. Name 3 things in the room that have the same color to pull your brain out of the void.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'internal desert' focus by forcing the eyes to scan and categorize the external environment.

social.text: Visual Resource. Look at a photo of a trusted person or pet. Acknowledge their existence quietly.
social.physioTip: Best for: Low-demand co-regulation using visual memories to refill the social 'tank' without requiring output.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You perceive a drained, barren world. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Breath Wave. Place one hand on your belly; feel the subtle rise and fall of breath for 5 cycles.
body.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a rhythmic internal anchor through gentle diaphragm movement.

mind.text: Object Description. Find one object and describe it in your head with three specific words.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Anchoring the thinking brain to a single, concrete reality to prevent the feeling of 'fading away'.

social.text: Self-Hug. Place your hands on opposite shoulders and squeeze firmly to remind your brain where your edges are.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing 'proprioceptive closure'—using deep pressure to define the body's physical boundaries.

text.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathB.text: Friction Heat. Rub your palms together quickly to generate heat, then place them on your cold cheeks or arms.

text.title: Empty
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel empty. You have given everything, and your system has now closed the doors to recharge.
text.blueprint: Post-stress collapse. Metabolic resources are at 'Reserve' levels; the brain is muting signals to prevent further drain.
text.physicalSensation: A hollow feeling in the solar plexus; a sensation of being a 'shell' with no internal substance.
text.metaphor: An empty warehouse after the move—echoey, quiet, and bare.
text.theImpulse: To lie flat, avoid conversation, and stare at a blank surface.
text.superpower: The Blank Slate. This emptiness is a prerequisite for a clean, new beginning once you have rested.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The void feels heavy and bottomless. Goal: Gentle Anchoring.

body.text: Skin Contact. Place a hand on your chest and feel the subtle warmth of your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the body's own warmth to signal safety and presence to the heart-center.

mind.text: Gentle Observance. Simply notice the sensation of air entering your nostrils. No counting, just noticing.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Anchoring awareness to the body's most basic survival function without adding cognitive load.

social.text: Ambient Companion. Look at a plant or neutral object and acknowledge it as a companion in the space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Breaking isolation through non-demanding, non-human connection.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel disconnected from your environment. Goal: Pull in.

body.text: Tactile Trace. Run your fingers along the texture of your clothing or the chair you are in.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using exteroception to define the physical boundary between 'self' and 'world'.

mind.text: Vocal Identity. State your name and your current location out loud in a whisper.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-orienting the 'Thinking Brain' to time and place after a period of dissociation.

social.text: Auditory Memory. Recall the sound of a safe person’s voice without needing to call them.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing co-regulation resources internally when external social energy is at zero.

text.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

text.title: Deflated
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel deflated. Your expectations met a roadblock, and your body dropped its tension to save battery.
text.blueprint: Loss of internal pressure. You feel like a sail with no wind—heavy, limp, and dragging.
text.physicalSensation: A lack of muscular 'tone'; shoulders and chest feel collapsed or hollowed out.
text.metaphor: A balloon that has lost its helium; it’s still there, but it’s hovering an inch off the floor, unable to rise.
text.theImpulse: To sigh deeply, drop your shoulders, and abandon the current task.
text.superpower: Efficiency. Deflation masterfully conserves your final sparks by folding inward like a protective husk.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and drained. Goal: Pull OUT.
pathA.body: Visual Lift. Use your eyes to trace the outline of a tall window or doorway to pull your posture up.
pathA.mind: Relative Scale. Look around and out loud name 3 things that are physically larger than you.
pathA.social: Ambient Prosody. Turn on a podcast of a comforting voice; let the rhythm play in the background.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel flat and are staring blankly into space. Goal: Pull IN.
pathB.body: Thermal Intake. Drink a glass of ice-cold water; focus entirely on the cold traveling down your throat.
pathB.mind: Internal Rhythm. Close your eyes and see if you can count 5 of your own heartbeats.
pathB.social: Battery Status. Text a safe person exactly what your internal battery percentage is right now.

text.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and drained. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Visual Lift. Use your eyes to trace the outline of a tall window or doorway to pull your posture up.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the vestibulo-ocular reflex to gently encourage a more upright, alert posture.

mind.text: Relative Scale. Look around and out loud name 3 things that are physically larger than you.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting focus from internal smallness to the stable, large-scale reality of your environment.

social.text: Ambient Prosody. Turn on a podcast of a comforting voice; let the rhythm play in the background.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the safety frequency of a human voice to provide a social 'buffer' without interaction.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel flat and are staring blankly into space. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Thermal Intake. Drink a glass of ice-cold water; focus entirely on the cold traveling down your throat.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a strong internal thermal signal to wake up the vagus nerve and anchor into the body.

mind.text: Internal Rhythm. Close your eyes and see if you can count 5 of your own heartbeats.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Tuning into the body's most basic internal clock to regain a sense of self.

social.text: Battery Status. Text a safe person exactly what your internal battery percentage is right now.
social.physioTip: Best for: Practicing objective self-reporting to a safe harbor without needing to 'explain' why.

pathB.text: Thermal Contrast. If you have a drink nearby, take a slow sip. If not, splash cold water on your wrists or use a cool cloth.

text.title: Frozen
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel frozen. Your system has detected a threat that feels inescapable, so it has crystallized your state to protect you from impact. You aren't 'stuck'; you are currently in a protective stasis.
text.blueprint: Biological crystallization. Your nervous system has 'iced over' to shield your core identity. This is a high-level defensive immobilization designed to wait out a storm.
text.physicalSensation: A rigid, icy stillness. You may feel cold in your hands and feet, a shallow 'locked' breath, and a heavy, immovable weight behind your eyes.
text.metaphor: An arctic landscape mid-winter. Everything is preserved in ice, motionless and silent, holding its breath until the spring.
text.theImpulse: To stay perfectly still, to stop time, and to avoid any movement that might 'break' the protective shell.
text.superpower: Preservation. You are currently a biological time-capsule. Your system is protecting your most precious internal resources from being touched by a harsh outside world.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are trapped in the internal ice. Goal: Internal Heat.
pathA.body: Gently rub your hands together to create a tiny bit of friction-warmth and place them over your heart.
pathA.mind: Visualize a single candle flame in the center of your chest, glowing steadily.
pathA.social: The Lighthouse. Imagine the face of a person who makes you feel safe. Just see them there.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels cold and unreachable. Goal: Sensory Spark.
pathB.body: Wrap your hands around a warm cup or hold a soft fabric against your cheek.
pathB.mind: Name one thing in the room that is physically 'soft' or 'round'.
pathB.social: Safe Proximity. If safe, ask a person or pet to sit in the same room as you, without talking.

text.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

body.physioTip: Best for: Using localized thermal input to signal 'the thaw' to the heart-center.

mind.text: The Internal Flame. Visualize a single candle flame in the center of your chest, glowing steadily.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using focused imagery to gently increase interoceptive warmth without physical exertion.

social.text: The Lighthouse. Imagine the face of a person who makes you feel safe. Just see them there.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing safe attachment figures internally to lower the 'threat-detection' response.

body.physioTip: Best for: Shifting the sensory context from 'frozen/rigid' to 'warm/malleable' using external textures.

mind.text: Soft Scanning. Name one thing in the room that is physically 'soft' or 'round'.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Training the eyes to look for 'safety cues' (organic, non-sharp shapes) in the environment.

social.text: Safe Proximity. If safe, ask a person or pet to sit in the same room as you, without talking.
social.physioTip: Best for: Experiencing co-presence and 'holding space' without the demand of verbal output.

pathB.text: The Internal Flame. Visualize a single candle flame in your chest. As you exhale slowly, imagine the warmth spreading to your shoulders.

text.title: Shutdown
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel shut down. Numbness isn't a lack of feeling; it’s a high-level protective gear your brain wears to prevent a total system crash. You are not broken; your body is successfully executing an ancient survival protocol.
text.blueprint: The 'Vault' response. Your system has determined the environment is too costly to engage with. It has pulled the emergency brake to protect your vital organs.
text.physicalSensation: A heavy, leaden weight throughout the body. A sense of being 'switched off' or disconnected from the motor controls.
text.metaphor: A deep-sea creature retreating into a dark trench where the pressure is immense, but the world above cannot reach it.
text.theImpulse: To curl into a ball, pull the covers over your head, and completely disappear from view.
text.superpower: Absolute Containment. You are currently a fortress. Your system is prioritizing core survival by cutting off all external noise.
text.generalGoal: The 'Thaw'. A tiny pulse of presence.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and trapped inside. Goal: Pull OUT.
pathA.body: Gently press your tongue against the roof of your mouth and feel the texture of your palate.
pathA.mind: Count backwards from 10 out loud to engage your vocal cords and the room.
pathA.social: Passive Safety. Listen to a podcast with a familiar, comforting voice to cue biological safety.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel spaced out and detached from your body. Goal: Pull IN.
pathB.body: Rub your hands together for 30 seconds to generate heat, then place them over your eyes.
pathB.mind: Notice one shadow in the room and trace how it falls on a surface.
pathB.social: Neutral Witness. Acknowledge a plant or chair as a neutral, safe presence in your space.

text.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and trapped inside. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Palate Press. Gently press your tongue against the roof of your mouth and feel the texture of your palate.
body.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating cranial nerves and internal awareness through low-effort oral proprioception.

mind.text: The Vocal Count. Count backwards from 10 out loud to engage your vocal cords and the room.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Activating the vocal apparatus to break the 'silent' shutdown state and re-occupy the space.

social.text: Passive Safety. Listen to a podcast with a familiar, comforting voice to cue biological safety.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using human prosody (voice tone) as a safe 'anchor' without the demand of a response.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel spaced out and detached from your body. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Thermal Palm-Press. Rub your hands together for 30 seconds to generate heat, then place them over your eyes.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using localized heat to soothe the optic nerve and pull awareness back into the physical self.

mind.text: Shadow Trace. Notice one shadow in the room and trace how it falls on a surface.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the visual system in a low-intensity, rhythmic scan of the current environment.

social.text: Neutral Witness. Acknowledge a plant or chair as a neutral, safe presence in your space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a sense of 'being seen' or 'being with' non-judgmental objects when human contact feels too loud.

pathB.text: Texture Scan. Notice the texture of one surface near you—the grain of wood, a weave of fabric, or a smooth wall.
Texture Scan. Notice the texture of one surface near you—the grain of wood, a weave of fabric, or a smooth wall.

text.title: Dimmed
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel dimmed. Your system has lowered the brightness to protect you from glare and overwhelm. You are safe to stay in low-resolution until your energy returns.
text.blueprint: Sensory gating. The brain is filtering out details to conserve metabolic 'bandwidth'.
text.physicalSensation: A sense of looking through a fog; sounds feel muffled; a lack of sharp edges in your perception.
text.metaphor: A screen that has entered 'Low Power Mode'—the content is there, but the colors are muted and the light is low.
text.theImpulse: To stare blankly, to avoid bright lights or loud noises, and to move slowly.
text.superpower: Bandwidth Protection. Your system is expertly managing its resources so you don't hit a total blackout.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal fog is thick. Goal: External Contrast.

body.text: Visual Sharpening. Find the sharpest corner of a piece of furniture and trace it with your eyes for 3 cycles.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast visual edges to wake up the focal system and cut through the 'fog'.

mind.text: The Color Count. Find and name 3 objects in the room that are the brightest color you can see.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to prioritize high-saturation signals to increase sensory input.

social.text: Voice Anchor. Listen to a single minute of a podcast or voice note from a trusted person.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the familiar prosody of a safe voice to re-orient to the social world.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels flat and distant. Goal: Internal Texture.

body.text: Texture Scan. Notice the texture of one surface near you—the grain of wood, a weave of fabric, or a smooth wall.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast tactile input to anchor the nervous system into the present moment.

mind.text: Weight Recognition. Notice exactly where your body is making contact with the chair or floor.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using gravity and pressure to define the 'self' when the sense of presence feels faint.

social.text: The Heart-Hand Anchor. Place one hand on your chest; notice the pressure of your clothes against your palm.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using self-touch as a low-demand social signal of safety and self-presence.

pathB.text: Thermal Intake. Take a slow sip of cold water and feel the temperature change in your throat.
Best for: Using internal thermal contrast to bypass the 'fog' and wake up the vagus nerve.

text.title: Numb
text.validation: It makes sense that you feel numb. Numbness is a biological anesthetic; your brain has turned down the volume on all input to save you from feeling too much at once.
text.blueprint: Sensory Dampening. The Dorsal Vagus is muting your feedback loops. This mechanism prevents emotional or physical overwhelm from reaching your processor.
text.physicalSensation: A 'cotton-wool' feeling in the head; a lack of tactile sensation in the skin; a feeling of being 'buffered' from the air.
text.metaphor: Walking through the world wearing a thick diving suit. You can see the world, but you can't feel the breeze.
text.theImpulse: To disconnect, to drift, and to remain in the 'gray' where nothing can touch you.
text.superpower: Impact Protection. You can exist in high-stress environments without absorbing the blow. You are in 'Armor' mode.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel lost in a gray void. Goal: Direct Sensation.
pathA.body: Grip your forearms firmly and squeeze. Feel the actual pressure of your own hands.
pathA.mind: The Boundary Scan. Describe the physical weight of your feet on the floor with 3 words.
pathA.social: Safe Ping. Text a safe person: 'Feeling a bit quiet.' No further explanation needed.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a silent movie. Goal: Sensory Tethers.
pathB.body: Tap a rhythm on a hard surface and listen closely to the 'click' sound.
pathB.mind: Pick an object and trace its outline with your eyes as if you were drawing it.
pathB.social: Memory Trace. Look at a photo of a pet and try to recall exactly how their fur feels.

text.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside

body.text: The Forearm Grip. Grip your forearms firmly and squeeze. Feel the actual pressure of your own hands.

mind.text: The Boundary Scan. Describe the physical weight of your feet on the floor with 3 words.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Anchoring the mind to the heaviest point of contact with the earth to counteract the feeling of drifting.

social.text: Safe Ping. Text a safe person: 'Feeling a bit quiet.' No further explanation needed.
social.physioTip: Best for: Maintaining social visibility without the high metabolic cost of a full conversation.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a silent movie. Goal: Sensory Tethers.

body.text: Rhythmic Tapping. Tap a rhythm on a hard surface and listen closely to the 'click' sound.
body.physioTip: Best for: Synchronizing hearing and touch to create a multi-channel anchor in the present moment.

mind.text: The Eye Trace. Pick an object and trace its outline with your eyes as if you were drawing it.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using active focal tracking to sharpen the 'low-resolution' perception typical of numbness.

social.text: Tactile Memory Trace. Look at a photo of a pet and try to recall exactly how their fur feels.
social.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the sensory cortex by 'simulating' touch through safe emotional memory.

pathB.pathB: Pressure Scan. Press your heels firmly into the ground and notice the resistance of the floor.
Best for: Creating a high-intensity pressure signal at the body's furthest point to re-establish the vertical axis.

pathB.title: Drained
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel drained. You've been running on 'E' for too long, and your system has forced a shutdown to prevent permanent engine damage.
pathB.blueprint: Resource Bankruptcy. Your sympathetic fuel is gone. Your body is now enforcing a mandatory metabolic recharge to prevent total collapse.
pathB.physicalSensation: Burning or aching in the muscles; dry eyes; a 'fuzzy' perimeter to your thoughts and vision.
pathB.metaphor: A smartphone battery at 1%. The screen is dimmed, apps are closed, and only emergency calls are possible.
pathB.theImpulse: To stop all decision-making, close your eyes mid-sentence, and sleep indefinitely.
pathB.superpower: Radical Honesty. Drained is the body’s most honest boundary. It is the wisdom that knows when 'more' is no longer possible.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the ache of depletion. Goal: Internal Softening.
pathA.body: Scan your jaw. If it's tight, give it permission to 'drop' just 2 millimeters.
pathA.mind: Permission Slip. Give yourself permission to think about 'nothing' for 60 seconds.
pathA.social: Shielding. Imagine a 'Do Not Disturb' sign hanging on your internal door.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels too bright and fast. Goal: Dimming the Input.
pathB.body: Cup your palms over your eyes to create total darkness for 20 seconds.
pathB.mind: Listen for the furthest sound you can hear—beyond the building.
pathB.social: Status Update. Text a safe person: 'Offline for a bit. Rest mode.' This protects your energy.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
