Best for: Switching from 'alert' focal vision to 'safe' peripheral vision to lower sympathetic arousal.

pathB.title: Lethargic
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel lethargic. Your system has shifted into a low-power motor mode to prevent you from over-extending.
pathB.blueprint: Metabolic Braking. The dorsal vagal system is slowing down your motor output to conserve what little energy is left.
pathB.physicalSensation: A heavy, glue-like feeling in the muscles. Movement feels like walking through deep mud.
pathB.metaphor: A clock with a battery so low that the hands are barely ticking. The mechanism is fine, but the current is weak.
pathB.theImpulse: To move as little as possible, speak in a monotone, and remain slumped or reclined.
pathB.superpower: Energy Safeguard. You are preventing a total system crash by moving only at a pace your battery can support.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and sluggish. Goal: External Pulse.
pathA.body: Slowly tap your toes against the floor, alternating left and right.
pathA.mind: The Metal Scan. Count 5 objects in the room made of metal.
pathA.social: Minimal Signal. Send a single emoji to a safe person just to stay tethered.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too fast and demanding. Goal: Internal Anchor.
pathB.body: Take a very small sip of water and notice the sensation of swallowing.
pathB.mind: Audio Scan. Name three things you can hear right now, no matter how quiet.
pathB.social: Safe Imagery. Imagine sitting by a slow-moving river, letting the world float past.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

body.text: The Swallowing Sip. Take a very small sip of water and notice the sensation of swallowing.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a high-sensitivity internal movement to reconnect with the midline of the body.

mind.text: Audio Scan. Name three things you can hear right now, no matter how quiet.

pathB.pathB: The Swallowing Sip. Take a small sip of water; track the sensation from your throat all the way to your stomach.
Best for: Engaging the autonomic nervous system's internal pathways to ground the self from the inside out.

pathB.title: Disconnected
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel disconnected. When the environment feels unsafe or hostile, your brain protects you by 'unplugging' the data feed. You aren't losing your mind; you are using a biological shield.
pathB.blueprint: Dissociative buffering. Your nervous system is muting the connection between your 'self' and your surroundings to reduce the emotional impact of a perceived threat.
pathB.physicalSensation: A feeling of being 'behind glass' or looking at the world from far away. Your own voice may sound distant, and your limbs might feel like they don't quite belong to you.
pathB.metaphor: A radio station with a weak signal—there is a lot of static, and the 'real' broadcast feels like it's happening in another room.
pathB.theImpulse: To stare blankly, to drift into daydreams, and to avoid eye contact or any interaction that requires 'presence'.
pathB.superpower: The Great Buffer. You have the ability to survive overwhelming environments by distancing your core self from the immediate chaos.
pathB.generalGoal: Tactile grounding. Finding one physical tether.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel lost in an internal fog or a 'void'. Goal: Physical Presence.
pathA.body: Grip the edges of your chair or your own forearms as firmly as you can for 5 seconds, then release.
pathA.mind: The Identity Anchor. State your full name, the current day of the week, and your location out loud in a clear whisper.
pathA.social: Hand Scan. Look at your own palm and trace the unique lines with one finger, acknowledging: 'This is my hand.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels fake or like a movie you aren't part of. Goal: Sensory Sharpness.
pathB.body: Hold an ice cube or something very cold for a moment to 'shock' the senses back to the current temperature.
pathB.mind: The Texture Hunt. Find one object and name its texture: Is it rough, smooth, cold, or soft?
pathB.social: Animal Connection. Look at a photo of a pet and try to recall exactly how their fur feels under your hand.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

body.text: The Thermal Shock. Hold an ice cube or something very cold for a moment to 'shock' the senses back to the current temperature.

mind.text: The Texture Hunt. Find one object and name its texture: Is it rough, smooth, cold, or soft?

social.text: Tactile Memory Trace. Look at a photo of a pet and try to recall exactly how their fur feels under your hand.

pathB.pathB: The Surface Press. Press your palms firmly against a cold wall or a hard table and notice the resistance.
Best for: Using external resistance and temperature to provide a clear 'edge' to where the body ends and the world begins.

pathB.title: Collapsed
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel collapsed. The demands became too heavy, and your body initiated a protective drop in energy to keep you safe. You are not weak; your biology is executing a damage-control protocol.
pathB.blueprint: Postural surrender. Your system has rapidly lost its sympathetic tone, causing your muscles to go slack to prevent a complete system fracture.
pathB.physicalSensation: A sinking feeling in the chest; slumped shoulders; a heavy chin. It feels like your bones have turned to lead and gravity has doubled.
pathB.metaphor: A tent that has lost its center pole in a storm; the fabric is still there, but the structure has given way to the weight of the rain.
pathB.theImpulse: To drop to the floor, curl into a ball, weep quietly, or surrender entirely to gravity.
pathB.superpower: Surrender. Your system wisely pulled the plug to prevent a break, allowing the storm to pass over you rather than through you.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel physically folded inward and heavy. Goal: Subtle Lift.
pathA.body: Slowly uncurl your spine just an inch, lift your chin, and use your eyes to trace the outline of a doorway or window.
pathA.mind: Acknowledge one 'constant'—something that hasn't changed or moved (e.g., the floor, the wall).
pathA.social: Safe Witness. Mentally name one person or animal who has seen you at your worst and didn't leave.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too bright, loud, or close. Goal: Pull IN.
pathB.body: Squeeze your right hand down your left arm, from shoulder to wrist. Repeat on the other side.
pathB.mind: Spine Sensing. Try to sense your spine, one vertebra at a time, from the tailbone up.
pathB.social: The Maintenance Sign. Imagine a 'Closed for Maintenance' sign on your door. Give yourself permission to be 'offline'.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

Best for: Feeling the internal pressure of the breath to provide a temporary 'internal pole' for the collapsed system.

pathB.title: Withdrawn
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel withdrawn. Your system is curating its energy by 'unplugging' from social demands. This is a strategic conservation of your remaining resources.
pathB.blueprint: Social engagement shutdown. Your nervous system has assessed that the 'cost' of interacting is too high, so it has pulled you into a protective inner sanctum.
pathB.physicalSensation: A feeling of being 'tucked in' or wearing an invisible cloak. You may notice your voice is quieter and your gaze shifts downward.
pathB.metaphor: A turtle that has pulled into its shell—not because it's mean, but because the world feels a bit too loud for its skin today.
pathB.theImpulse: To cancel plans, move to a smaller room, and avoid being 'seen' or 'needed'.
pathB.superpower: Deep Introspection. In this shell, you have the quietest environment possible to hear your own internal truth.

pathA.social: The Window. Go sit by a window and watch cars or birds pass. Zero interaction required.

pathB.title: Sinking
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are sinking. When progress feels stalled, your body lets go of its 'upward' tension to save fuel for the long haul.
pathB.blueprint: Gravity surrender. Your sympathetic 'up' energy has dipped, allowing you to descend into a heavy, metabolic rest state.
pathB.physicalSensation: A dragging feeling in the pelvis and legs, as if your chair is made of quicksand. Your chest feels heavy and your breath feels deep and slow.
pathB.metaphor: An anchor slowly descending into deep water—away from the turbulent surface and into the stillness of the bottom.
pathB.theImpulse: To stop trying, to let your body go limp, and to slide down until you can't go any lower.
pathB.superpower: Finding the Floor. You cannot sink forever; eventually, you hit the bottom, and that becomes the solid ground you can push off from.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside

pathA.body: Inhale and imagine your ribs expanding sideways like an accordion, just for one breath.
pathA.mind: Buoyancy Imagery. Imagine one thing that naturally floats (a balloon, a cork, a cloud).
pathA.social: The Lift. Think of one person who always lifts your mood just by being themselves.

pathA.goal: You are descending into heavy internal depth. Goal: Micro-Expansion.

body.text: The Accordion Breath. Inhale and imagine your ribs expanding sideways like an accordion, just for one breath.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the diaphragm to create 'internal volume' and counteract the sinking feeling in the chest.

mind.text: Buoyancy Imagery. Imagine one thing that naturally floats (a balloon, a cork, a cloud).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain with 'upward' concepts to gently shift the metabolic tone away from descent.

social.text: The Lift. Think of one person who always lifts your mood just by being themselves.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing safe attachment memories to spark a tiny pulse of sympathetic 'up' energy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel disconnected and adrift. Goal: Physical Support.

body.text: The Heel Press. Press your heels firmly into the ground and feel the floor 'pushing back' against you.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating the posterior chain muscles to find 'the bottom' and establish a solid base for future movement.

mind.text: Line Finding. Find one horizontal line in the room (a shelf, a floorboard) and follow it with your eyes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using stable environmental horizons to settle a dizzy or 'adrift' nervous system.

social.text: Weight. Ask a safe person for a firm hand-on-the-shoulder or use a weighted blanket to feel 'contained'.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing deep pressure input to the shoulders to signal that you are physically supported and 'held'.

pathB.pathB: The Heavy Sink. Let your weight drop fully into the chair for 3 breaths, then notice where you feel the most support.
Best for: Surrendering to the sink fully until the system registers that the floor/chair is successfully holding you.

pathB.title: Exhausted
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel exhausted. Your system is ringing the 'empty' bell to prevent you from doing permanent damage to your internal engine.
pathB.blueprint: Metabolic bankruptcy. You have spent more than you have earned, and your system is enforcing a mandatory 'spending freeze'.
pathB.physicalSensation: A burning or aching in the muscles; heavy, dry eyes; a 'fuzzy' or blurred perimeter to your thoughts.
pathB.metaphor: A runner who has crossed the finish line and simply dropped to the grass. The race is over; the recovery has begun.
pathB.theImpulse: To close your eyes mid-sentence, to stop all decision-making, and to sleep for an indefinite amount of time.
pathB.superpower: The Biological Truth. Exhaustion is your body's most honest boundary. It is the wisdom that knows when 'more' is no longer possible.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the internal ache of depletion. Goal: Internal Softening.
pathA.body: Scan your jaw and belly. If they are tight, give them permission to 'drop' for 5 seconds.
pathA.mind: Zero-Tasking. Give yourself permission to think about 'absolutely nothing' for one minute.
pathA.social: The Privacy Shield. Imagine a 'Do Not Disturb' sign hanging on your chest.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too bright and fast for your tired eyes. Goal: Dimming the Input.
pathB.body: Cup your palms over your eyes to create total darkness for 30 seconds.
pathB.mind: Audio Distance. Listen for the furthest sound you can hear—beyond the room, beyond the building.
pathB.social: The Out-of-Office. Text a safe person: 'Rest mode on. Offline for a while.' No reply needed.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the internal ache of depletion. Goal: Internal Softening.

body.text: The Soft Drop. Scan your jaw and belly. If they are tight, give them permission to 'drop' for 5 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Releasing 'bracing' patterns in the core and TMJ to signal the body that the effort phase is over.

mind.text: Zero-Tasking. Give yourself permission to think about 'absolutely nothing' for one minute.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Halting the metabolic cost of cognitive processing and executive decision-making.

social.text: The Privacy Shield. Imagine a 'Do Not Disturb' sign hanging on your chest.
social.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the 'social scanning' reflex that drains energy when in the presence of others.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too bright and fast for your tired eyes. Goal: Dimming the Input.

body.text: Eye Palming. Cup your palms over your eyes to create total darkness for 30 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Giving the optic nerve a total break, which immediately lowers sympathetic arousal in the brain.

mind.text: Audio Distance. Listen for the furthest sound you can hear—beyond the room, beyond the building.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from focal, high-effort attention to a passive, expansive auditory awareness.

social.text: The Out-of-Office. Text a safe person: 'Rest mode on. Offline for a while.' No reply needed.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using active communication to protect your energy, removing the 'guilt' of non-responsiveness.

pathB.pathB: The Weighted Blanket. Notice the weight of your clothes or a blanket on your body and let it feel 5% heavier.
Best for: Using gravity as a passive 'grounding' force to settle a vibrating, tired nervous system.

pathB.title: Fading
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are fading. When the world feels hostile and your energy is low, your system begins to dim your presence to make you less of a target.
pathB.blueprint: Hypo-arousal transition. Your nervous system is lowering the 'brightness' of your consciousness and motor drive because it perceives that being fully present is too risky or exhausting.
pathB.physicalSensation: A sense of becoming 'translucent' or light-headed. Vision may feel slightly soft or out of focus, and your internal 'voice' feels quiet and far away.
pathB.metaphor: A sunset behind a thick bank of clouds; the light is still there, but the colors are draining away into a dull gray.
pathB.theImpulse: To stop talking mid-sentence, to drift away from the conversation, and to physically move toward the shadows or the edge of the room.
pathB.superpower: Stealth Preservation. You are expertly minimizing your 'biological footprint' to wait for a safer time to shine.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel like you are disappearing into your own mind. Goal: Solidify the Center.
pathA.body: Stomp your feet on the floor twice, quite hard. Feel the vibration travel up to your knees.
pathA.mind: The Sharp Focus. Find one object with text and read the smallest word you can see.
pathA.social: Say your own name out loud in a firm, clear voice.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's drifting away from you. Goal: Sensory Re-attachment.
pathB.body: Wash your hands with cold water and focus entirely on the sensation of the water hitting your skin.
pathB.mind: Count the number of right angles (corners) you can see in your current view.
pathB.social: Look at a photo of a safe person and mentally list three specific things you like about their face.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel like you are disappearing into your own mind. Goal: Solidify the Center.

body.text: The Ground Stomp. Stomp your feet on the floor twice, quite hard. Feel the vibration travel up to your knees.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-impact vibration to 'wake up' the lower limbs and anchor the self into the ground.

mind.text: Micro-Reading. Find one object with text and read the smallest word you can see.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the eyes into 'focal vision' to interrupt the soft-focus drift of fading.

social.text: The Name Call. Say your own name out loud in a firm, clear voice.
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing personal identity and presence through the vibration of your own vocal cords.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's drifting away from you. Goal: Sensory Re-attachment.

body.text: The Temperature Reset. Wash your hands with cold water and focus entirely on the sensation of the water hitting your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using thermal shock to bypass the 'fading' consciousness and force a sensory re-entry.

mind.text: Corner Count. Count the number of right angles (corners) you can see in your current view.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the geometric-analytical brain to provide structure when reality feels 'soft' or blurry.

social.text: Feature Focus. Look at a photo of a safe person and mentally list three specific things you like about their face.
social.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from vague isolation to high-detail relational connection using safe visual cues.

pathB.title: Slumped
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel slumped. Your system has stopped 'holding' you upright against a world that feels heavy and discouraging.
pathB.blueprint: Postural deflation. The sympathetic 'tone' that keeps our spine erect has been turned down by the dorsal vagus, allowing gravity to take the lead.
pathB.physicalSensation: Curved shoulders, a drooping head, and a heavy pelvis. It feels like your muscles have lost their 'spring' and are just hanging off your bones.
pathB.metaphor: A sunflower at the end of a long, rain-heavy day—bowing its head because the weight of the water is more than it can carry.
pathB.theImpulse: To slide down in your chair, to rest your head in your hands, and to sigh deeply while looking at the floor.
pathB.superpower: Yielding to Gravity. You are resting the very muscles that usually have to work the hardest. You are letting the earth hold you for a moment.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel physically and emotionally folded in. Goal: Subtle Expansion.
pathA.body: Simply turn your palms upward to face the ceiling. Notice how this slightly opens your shoulders.
pathA.mind: Imagine a string attached to the very top of your head, gently pulling you upward by just one millimeter.
pathA.social: Visualize a friend giving you a gentle, supportive hand between your shoulder blades.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's pressing down on your back. Goal: Horizon Expansion.
pathB.body: Slowly lift your gaze from the floor to the furthest point you can see in the room or out a window.
pathB.mind: Identify three things in the room that are physically 'high up' (a lamp, a shelf, a picture).
pathB.social: Listen to a song with a steady, uplifting beat and try to tap your finger to it.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel physically and emotionally folded in. Goal: Subtle Expansion.

body.text: The Palm Flip. Simply turn your palms upward to face the ceiling. Notice how this slightly opens your shoulders.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the kinetic chain of the arm to naturally externally rotate the humerus and open the chest without muscular strain.

mind.text: The Crown String. Imagine a string attached to the very top of your head, gently pulling you upward by just one millimeter.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using ideokinesis to re-engage deep postural stabilizers along the spine without triggering a 'pushing' response.

social.text: The Shoulder Support. Visualize a friend giving you a gentle, supportive hand between your shoulder blades.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing safe tactile memory to provide a symbolic 'pillar' of support for the collapsed upper back.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's pressing down on your back. Goal: Horizon Expansion.

body.text: The Horizon Lift. Slowly lift your gaze from the floor to the furthest point you can see in the room or out a window.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the eyes to lead the cervical spine into extension, signaling the brain to shift from 'threat-hiding' to 'horizon-scanning'.

mind.text: High-Point Scan. Identify three things in the room that are physically 'high up' (a lamp, a shelf, a picture).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'downward gaze' habit of morose states by forcing the eyes to move vertically.

social.text: The Rhythmic Tap. Listen to a song with a steady, uplifting beat and try to tap your finger to it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Entraining the nervous system to an external rhythm to pull the motor system out of a 'stalled' state.

pathB.pathB: Wait, Verena, they have too much motor energy for that—let's suggest a Wall Push or a fast exhale instead to discharge the heat first.

pathB.title: Shrinking
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are shrinking. Making yourself smaller is a brilliant biological way to reduce your exposure to stress.
pathB.blueprint: Protective Coiling. Your nervous system is signaling your muscles to pull inward toward your core (the 'fetal' tendency) to protect your vital organs from perceived social or emotional pressure.
pathB.physicalSensation: Tightness in the chest, pulling the knees together, and tucking the chin. You feel 'small' inside your clothes.
pathB.metaphor: A piece of wool shrinking in hot water; becoming tighter, denser, and less expansive.
pathB.theImpulse: To cross your arms, hide your hands, and avoid taking up space in the room.
pathB.superpower: Core Protection. You are masterfully guarding your most vulnerable parts. You are becoming a 'harder target' while you regroup.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel tight and small internally. Goal: Width Awareness.
pathA.body: Stretch your arms out wide to the sides, as if you were measuring a large box, then let them drop.
pathA.mind: Say silently: 'I am allowed to take up this much space.'
pathA.social: Recall a time you felt powerful or 'big' (e.g., after a workout or a success).

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels too big or intimidating. Goal: Spatial Grounding.
pathB.body: Place your hands on the outer edges of your thighs and push outward against them.
pathB.mind: Count how many steps it would take to walk to the nearest wall.
pathB.social: Find one object in the room that looks 'solid' and 'unshakeable'. Focus on it for 10 seconds.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside

body.text: The Wing Span. Stretch your arms out wide to the sides, as if you were measuring a large box, then let them drop.
body.physioTip: Best for: Gently interrupting flexor dominance by activating the extensor muscles of the upper back and shoulders.

mind.text: Space Permission. Say silently: 'I am allowed to take up this much space.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the cognitive 'shame' response that often accompanies the physical urge to hide.

social.text: Power Recall. Recall a time you felt powerful or 'big' (e.g., after a workout or a success).
social.physioTip: Best for: Using somatic memory to re-ignite a sense of agency and physical presence.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels too big or intimidating. Goal: Spatial Grounding.

body.text: The Outer Thigh Press. Place your hands on the outer edges of your thighs and push outward against them.
body.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the lateral line of the body to provide a sense of 'sturdy' external boundaries.

mind.text: Distance Estimation. Count how many steps it would take to walk to the nearest wall.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-calculating your relationship to the physical environment to lower the 'intimidation' of the space.

social.text: The Anchor Object. Find one object in the room that looks 'solid' and 'unshakeable'. Focus on it for 10 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Outsourcing stability to an external object when the internal sense of 'solidness' is low.

pathB.title: Heavy
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel heavy or sad. Your body is lowering your pace so you can process loss or disappointment without crashing. You are not broken. Your system is taking a necessary pause to process and heal.
pathB.blueprint: Metabolic conservation. Your system has slowed your heart rate and muscle tone to help you endure emotional weight, prioritizing internal 'processing' over external 'doing'.
pathB.physicalSensation: A dragging weight in the limbs; a 'thick' feeling in the air; the sensation that your shoes are made of lead.
pathB.metaphor: Walking through knee-deep water or wearing a lead vest. Every movement requires a conscious decision and extra fuel.
pathB.theImpulse: To let the head hang, let the shoulders drop, move slowly, sigh often, and seek a soft place to land.
pathB.superpower: Depth and grounding. This heaviness forces a pause that allows for deep metabolic and emotional processing. You are honoring what matters.
pathB.generalGoal: Gentle lift. Lighten the load. Subtle unburdening.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel weighed down by a thick, internal cloud of sadness. Goal: Pull OUT.
pathA.body: Press palms flat on a table, push down firmly while lifting your chest slightly—feel the counterforce expand your posture.
pathA.mind: Texture Anchor. Silently name the exact texture of the fabric touching your skin right now (rough, soft, cool, fuzzy).
pathA.social: The Appreciation Scrap. Write down one thing you appreciate about a loved one on a scrap of paper.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels gray, like a distant movie you aren't part of. Goal: Pull IN.
pathB.body: Breath Wave. Place a hand on your chest and one on your belly. Feel the rise and fall of your breath for 5 cycles.
pathB.mind: Sensory Detail. Focus on one sound in the room and try to describe its 'shape' in your mind.
pathB.social: The Self-Hug. Give yourself a gentle, firm squeeze on the opposite arms or shoulders.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel weighed down by a thick, internal cloud of sadness. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Counter-Press. Press palms flat on a table, push down firmly while lifting your chest slightly—feel the counterforce expand your posture.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the ground reaction force to re-engage postural extensors and create 'buoyancy' in the ribcage.

mind.text: Texture Anchor. Silently name the exact texture of the fabric touching your skin right now (rough, soft, cool, fuzzy).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting focus from heavy internal emotions to immediate, neutral tactile data.

social.text: The Mental Scrap. Mentally name one specific thing you appreciate about a loved one or a pet.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'micro-gratitude' to spark a tiny pulse of oxytocin, softening the internal weight.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels gray, like a distant movie you aren't part of. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Breath Wave. Place a hand on your chest and one on your belly. Feel the rise and fall of your breath for 5 cycles.
body.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing a rhythmic internal anchor to counteract the feeling of being 'adrift' or disconnected.

mind.text: Sound Shape. Focus on one sound in the room and try to describe its 'shape' in your mind (e.g., sharp, round, fuzzy).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the associative cortex to transform passive hearing into active, grounding exteroception.

social.text: The Self-Squeeze. Give yourself a gentle, firm squeeze on the opposite arms or shoulders.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'somatic containment' to define the body's edges when the world feels too vast or gray.

pathB.title: Flat
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel flat or bored. Your brain is successfully recognizing a lack of meaningful stimulation and is prompting you to seek a better use of your energy. You are not lazy.
pathB.blueprint: Under-stimulation response. Your nervous system has assessed the environment as lacking reward or novelty, causing a mild drop in energy to 'idle' mode.
pathB.physicalSensation: A lack of 'spark' or appetite for any activity. Eyes feel slightly glazed, and your posture feels neutral but uninspired.
pathB.metaphor: A car idling in a driveway, burning fuel without going anywhere, or a radio tuned to a station playing nothing but quiet static.
pathB.theImpulse: To scroll mindlessly, seek quick dopamine (junk food), or disengage completely from the present moment.
pathB.superpower: Catalyst for Change. Boredom is a biological signal that you are ready for more. It is the friction that prompts creativity and movement.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel apathetic and unmotivated. Goal: Pull OUT.
pathA.body: The Full-Body Stretch. Reach high for the ceiling, then drop to touch your toes to circulate blood flow.
pathA.mind: Novelty Search. Find one object you haven't looked at in a long time and notice three brand-new details about it.
pathA.social: The Curiosity Text. Send a quick message to a friend asking about a topic they love.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are mindlessly searching the external world for a dopamine hit. Goal: Pull IN.
pathB.body: The Heat Press. Rub your palms together for 5 seconds to create heat, then press them firmly against your chest.
pathB.mind: The Location Scan. Try to locate exactly where the feeling of 'flatness' lives in your body (e.g., throat, eyes).
pathB.social: The Laughter Memory. Recall a time you laughed until it hurt. Try to feel that laughter in your belly for 10 seconds.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel apathetic and unmotivated. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Seated Twist. While sitting, rotate your torso gently to look over one shoulder, then the other.
body.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the spinal nerves and mechanoreceptors to 'wake up' the system without taxing low energy reserves.

mind.text: Novelty Search. Find one object you haven't looked at in a long time and notice three brand-new details about it.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-engaging the prefrontal cortex and dopamine pathways through the intentional search for novelty.

social.text: The Interest Ping. Send a quick message to a friend asking about a topic they are passionate about.
social.physioTip: Best for: Shifting focus toward external social narratives to break the loop of internal apathy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are mindlessly searching the external world for a dopamine hit. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Heat Press. Rub your palms together for 5 seconds to create heat, then press them firmly against your chest.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using thermal input to ground awareness back into the physical container, interrupting the 'mindless drift'.

mind.text: The Sensation Scan. Locate exactly where the feeling of 'flatness' lives in your body (e.g., throat, eyes, chest).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Transforming a vague 'mood' into a specific physical sensation, which makes it easier for the brain to process.

social.text: The Laughter Memory. Recall a time you laughed until it hurt. Try to feel that laughter in your belly for 10 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing somatic memory to trigger a minor release of endorphins and soften emotional flatness.

pathB.title: Retreating
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like retreating. Your system is identifying a lack of safety and is proactively pulling back to prevent further exposure. You are not being 'difficult'; you are being protective.
pathB.blueprint: Proactive withdrawal. A mild dorsal vagal shift where the body prepares to shut the gates before a full drain occurs.
pathB.physicalSensation: A subtle pulling back in the chest, shoulders turning away from the center, and a desire to look at the exit.
pathB.metaphor: The tide pulling away from the shore, leaving the sand exposed and quiet.
pathB.theImpulse: To leave the room, stop the conversation, or physically turn your back to the environment.
pathB.superpower: Boundaried Wisdom. You are noticing a lack of safety early, allowing you to choose your exit before your battery hits zero.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the urge to hide within yourself. Goal: Internal Fortification.
pathA.body: Cross your arms and tuck your hands into your armpits. Feel the containment of your own frame.
pathA.mind: The Invisible Wall. Imagine a clear, solid glass wall between you and whatever feels 'too much'.
pathA.social: The No-Reply. Give yourself permission to not respond to the last thing said for 30 seconds.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels repelling. Goal: Selective Orienting.
pathB.body: Find one object in the room that is 'neutral'—neither good nor bad—and stare at it for 10 seconds.
pathB.mind: Count the number of legs on the furniture in your immediate view.
pathB.social: Step back physically by one foot. Notice if that tiny bit of extra space feels better.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathB.pathB: I'm leaving before I get hurt.

social.text: The 30-Second Pause. Give yourself permission to not respond to the last thing said for 30 seconds.

pathB.title: Sagging
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel sagging. The internal pressure that holds you upright has dipped slightly, inviting you to lower your metabolic output.
pathB.blueprint: Mild postural release. Your sympathetic tone is fluctuating, allowing gravity to win the battle for your shoulders and chin.
pathB.physicalSensation: Heavy eyelids, a slight drooping of the mouth, and a feeling like your clothes are heavier than usual.
pathB.metaphor: A sail that has lost just enough wind to start fluttering and drooping, but the boat is still moving slowly.
pathB.theImpulse: To lean your head on your hand, to slump in your chair, and to speak in a quieter, flatter tone.
pathB.superpower: Micro-Rest. You are catching small pockets of recovery in the middle of the day, preventing a deeper collapse later.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the 'glum' weight internally. Goal: Soft Upward Pulse.
pathA.body: Inhale and imagine the air filling the space between your shoulder blades, lifting them slightly.
pathA.mind: The Gentle Question. Ask yourself: 'What is one tiny thing that would feel slightly better right now?'
pathA.social: Visualize a safe person giving you a quick, encouraging nod.

pathB.social: The 'Small Talk' Pivot. If with someone, ask a very simple, low-stakes question like 'What's the weather like out there?'

pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel dulled. Disappointment acts like a dampener on the senses, protecting you from the 'sharpness' of a recent letdown.
pathB.blueprint: Sensory muting. Your nervous system is slightly desensitizing your inputs to help you process a frustration without it feeling overwhelming.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'fuzzy' or muffled quality to sounds and colors. A feeling of being slightly behind a thin veil.
pathB.metaphor: Looking through a window that has a layer of dust on it—the view is there, but the edges are soft and the colors are dim.
pathB.theImpulse: To stop looking for the 'good' in things and to settle for a neutral, muted experience.
pathB.superpower: Disappointment Buffer. You are giving your heart time to adjust to a change in plans by not feeling the full 'sting' all at once.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel internal 'static' or dullness. Goal: Sensory Sharpness.
pathA.body: Gently pinch the skin on the back of your hand. Notice the sharp, clear sensation of the touch.
pathA.mind: Name three specific things you can see that are exactly the same color.
pathA.social: Write one word that describes your disappointment on a piece of paper, then fold it up.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels uninteresting or flat. Goal: Detail Orienting.
pathB.body: Touch something cold (metal) and something warm (fabric) and notice the difference.
pathB.mind: Read the longest word you can find in the room.
pathB.social: Notice the texture of the person’s clothing sitting nearest to you.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel internal 'static' or dullness. Goal: Sensory Sharpness.

body.text: The Cutaneous Ping. Gently pinch the skin on the back of your hand. Notice the sharp, clear sensation of the touch.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast tactile input to 'pierce' through sensory muting and re-alert the somatosensory cortex.

mind.text: Color Matching. Name three specific things you can see that are exactly the same color.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the visual system to engage in a specific search-and-sort task, which increases cognitive 'resolution'.

social.text: The Verbal Seal. Whisper one word that describes your disappointment, then take a slow breath.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the vibration of the voice to externalize and 'contain' an emotion without the demand of a full conversation.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels uninteresting or flat. Goal: Detail Orienting.

body.text: Thermal Contrast. Touch something cold (metal) and something warm (fabric) and notice the difference.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing temperature receptors to provide strong, distinct signals to the brain that interrupt the 'flat' feeling.

mind.text: The Long Word Hunt. Read the longest word you can find in the room.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the language-processing centers of the brain to shift focus from emotional 'fuzziness' to concrete data.

social.text: Texture Observation. Notice the texture of the clothing of someone nearby (e.g., knit, smooth, denim).
social.physioTip: Best for: Engaging in 'passive social curiosity' to stay tethered to the social world without requiring active output.

pathB.title: Sluggish
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel sluggish. Your system is moving slowly to carefully navigate a situation where you feel a bit guarded or uncertain.
pathB.blueprint: High-viscosity movement. Your system is increasing the 'resistance' in your motor circuits to ensure you don't move faster than your current sense of safety allows.
pathB.physicalSensation: Movement feels 'thick'. Your thoughts are moving at a slower tempo, and your reaction time feels slightly delayed.
pathB.metaphor: An engine running with oil that is a bit too thick; it works, but every gear shift takes a bit more effort.
pathB.theImpulse: To take the easiest path, to procrastinate, and to move in 'slow motion'.
pathB.superpower: Deliberate Pace. By moving slowly, you are less likely to make a mistake or miss a subtle cue in your environment.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'slow' and heavy internally. Goal: Rhythmic Pulse.
pathA.body: Tap your fingers on your lap to the rhythm of a song you like.
pathA.mind: Count your steps if you move, or count your breaths if you stay still.
pathA.social: Text a friend a 'slow' animal emoji (🐢, 🦥) to acknowledge your current pace.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's moving too fast. Goal: Synchronizing.
pathB.body: Follow a moving object with your eyes (a clock hand, a bird, a car) for 15 seconds.
pathB.mind: Describe one process happening in the room (e.g., 'The light is hitting the table').
pathB.social: Ask someone to repeat the last thing they said, just to give your brain time to catch up.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'slow' and heavy internally. Goal: Rhythmic Pulse.

body.text: The Bilateral Tap. Tap your fingers on your lap, alternating left and right, to a steady rhythm.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic bilateral stimulation to synchronize the brain hemispheres and reduce motor 'viscosity'.

mind.text: The Counted Breath. Count your breaths for 5 cycles: inhale (1), exhale (2), until you reach 10.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a simple, low-load cognitive track to follow, reducing the effort of internal decision-making.

social.text: The Pace Ping. Text a friend a 'slow' animal emoji (🐢, 🦥) to acknowledge your current tempo.
social.physioTip: Best for: Honoring your current metabolic speed in a social context, which removes the 'speed-up' pressure.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's moving too fast. Goal: Synchronizing.

body.text: Smooth Pursuit. Follow a moving object with your eyes (a clock hand, a bird, a car) for 15 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Engaging 'smooth pursuit' eye movements to stabilize the visual field and calm the brainstem.

mind.text: Process Description. Silently describe one simple physical process happening in the room (e.g., 'The fan is spinning').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Anchoring the 'Thinking Brain' to objective reality to prevent it from falling behind the external pace.

social.text: The Buffer Request. Ask someone to repeat the last thing they said, allowing your brain time to fully 'process' the data.
social.physioTip: Best for: Creating a social 'buffer' that allows for neuro-synchronization when processing speeds are lowered.

pathB.title: Foggy
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel foggy. Apathy is a way of protecting yourself from 'caring' too much when you feel vulnerable or over-exposed.
pathB.blueprint: Cognitive clouding. A mild dissociative state where the brain reduces the 'resolution' of your thoughts to prevent emotional overwhelm.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'cloud' behind the forehead. Difficulty finding specific words and a sense of being 'spaced out' while still awake.
pathB.metaphor: Driving through a light mist; you can see the road, but the signs are hard to read until you are right on top of them.
pathB.theImpulse: To shrug your shoulders, to say 'I don't know' or 'I don't care', and to avoid complex tasks.
pathB.superpower: Mental Buffer. The fog acts like a soft cushion, preventing sharp thoughts or criticisms from landing too hard while you are feeling vulnerable.
pathB.generalGoal: Clearing the windshield. One clear point.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'cloudy' and disconnected from your goals. Goal: Simple Clarity.
pathA.body: Take a deep breath and exhale with a loud 'Ha!' sound. Feel the air leave your lungs.
pathA.mind: The One-Task Anchor. Identify the single most simple thing you need to do next (e.g., 'Drink water').
pathA.social: State one true fact about your current environment out loud: 'The walls are white.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels blurry and uninteresting. Goal: Sharp Contrast.
pathB.body: Look at something very close (your palm) and then something very far away (out the window). Repeat 3 times.
pathB.mind: Find one object with a very sharp edge and trace it with your eyes.
pathB.social: Focus on the literal color of someone's eyes or hair for 5 seconds.

pathB.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'cloudy' and disconnected from your goals. Goal: Simple Clarity.

body.text: The Clearing Breath. Take a deep breath and exhale with a loud 'Ha!' sound. Feel the air leave your lungs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a forced exhale and vocalization to stimulate the diaphragm and break through respiratory 'stagnation'.

mind.text: The One-Task Anchor. Identify the single most simple thing you need to do next (e.g., 'Drink water').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing cognitive load by collapsing complex 'foggy' thoughts into a single, actionable binary task.

social.text: The Literal Fact. State one true fact about your current environment out loud: 'The walls are white.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'Thinking Brain' to label objective reality, which helps pull consciousness out of a dissociative drift.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels blurry and uninteresting. Goal: Sharp Contrast.

body.text: Near-Far Accommodation. Look at something very close (your palm) and then something very far away (out the window). Repeat 3 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the ciliary muscles and the ocular system to force the brain to 'update' its visual resolution.

mind.text: Sharp Edge Trace. Find one object with a very sharp edge and trace it with your eyes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast visual data to 'sharpen' the boundaries between objects and the self.

social.text: The Color Note. Focus on the literal color of a safe person’s eyes or hair for 5 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a micro-connection through neutral, high-detail observation without the stress of interaction.

pathB.title: Exploding
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are exploding. Your system has detected a critical boundary violation or threat and has flooded your engine with every drop of high-octane fuel to ensure your survival.
pathB.blueprint: Sympathetic Overload. The amygdala has hijacked the steering wheel, shutting down logic to provide maximum power to your limbs and lungs for an immediate fight-or-flight response.
pathB.physicalSensation: Extreme heat in the face and chest; a racing heart that feels like it might burst; jaw muscles clenched; and an overwhelming urge to move or strike.
pathB.metaphor: A solar flare or a steam boiler where the pressure gauge has hit the red line and the safety valve is screaming.
pathB.theImpulse: To strike out, to scream at the top of your lungs, or to physically destroy the perceived obstacle in your path.
pathB.superpower: Total Mobilization. You have access to a level of raw, explosive power that can move mountains. Your body is ready to protect you at any cost.
pathB.generalGoal: Mechanical cooling. Discharge the heat immediately.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are consumed by internal heat and a pounding heart. Goal: Cool the Engine.
pathA.body: Physiological Sigh. Take two sharp inhales through the nose, followed by one long, slow exhale through the mouth. Repeat 5 times.
pathA.mind: Panoramic Vision. Stop fixating on one point. Look up and soften your gaze to take in the widest possible view of the room.
pathA.social: The Boundary Declaration. If safe, say out loud: 'I am running too hot to talk. I am stepping away for 5 minutes to reset.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are hyper-fixated on the trigger. Goal: Break the Lock.
pathB.body: Wall Push. Place both hands flat against a wall and push with 100% of your strength for 10 seconds. Feel the resistance in your muscles.
pathB.mind: Neutral Scanning. Find and name 5 blue objects in the room. Focus only on the color, not the objects themselves.
pathB.social: The Vent Valve. Ask a safe person: 'I have massive energy. Just listen to me vent for 60 seconds without trying to fix it.'

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are consumed by internal heat and a pounding heart. Goal: Cool the Engine.

body.text: The Physiological Sigh. Take two sharp inhales through the nose, followed by one long, slow exhale through the mouth. Repeat 5 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Rapidly offloading CO2 and activating the vagus nerve to mechanically lower the heart rate in seconds.

mind.text: Panoramic Vision. Stop fixating on one point. Look up and soften your gaze to take in the widest possible view of the room.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'threat-tunnel vision' to peripheral vision, which sends a direct hardware signal to the brainstem to down-regulate.

social.text: The Boundary Declaration. If safe, say out loud: 'I am running too hot to talk. I am stepping away for 5 minutes to reset.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Exercising social agency to prevent a relationship 'collision' while in a high-arousal state.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are hyper-fixated on the trigger. Goal: Break the Lock.

body.text: The Isometric Wall Push. Place both hands flat against a wall and push with 100% of your strength for 10 seconds. Release and breathe.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging aggressive motor energy into a neutral, immovable object to simulate a 'fight' completion without destruction.

mind.text: Neutral Scanning. Find and name 5 blue objects in the room. Focus only on the color, not the objects themselves.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the prefrontal cortex back online by engaging in a cold, analytical categorization task.

social.text: The Energy Vent. Ask a safe person: 'I have massive energy right now. Can I just shake it out or vent while you stay near?'
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing co-regulation to anchor the explosive energy without needing to 'fix' the trigger immediately.

pathB.title: Flooded
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel flooded. Your sensory inputs are overflowing, and your brain can no longer process the 'data'—it only sees 'danger'. You are not losing control; you are being overwhelmed by your own survival energy.
pathB.blueprint: Sensory Saturation. Your nervous system is receiving more input than your 'processor' can handle, leading to a state of high-arousal confusion and panic.
pathB.physicalSensation: Short, shallow breaths; a feeling of 'pins and needles' or tingling; dizziness; and a sense that the room is spinning or closing in.
pathB.metaphor: A river that has burst its banks during a storm. The water is everywhere, and the current is too fast to stand in.
pathB.theImpulse: To run blindly, to flail, to hyperventilate, and to find an immediate exit at any cost.
pathB.superpower: Hyper-Vigilance. In this state, you are noticing everything. Your system is trying to find a gap in the chaos to lead you toward safety.
pathB.generalGoal: Lowering the water level. Rhythmic anchoring.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are drowning in your own internal sensations. Goal: Grounding the Pulse.
pathA.body: Stomp your feet. Feel the impact of your soles hitting the ground. Rhythmic, steady, solid.
pathA.mind: The 5-4-3-2-1 Game. Name 5 things you see, 4 you can touch, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste.
pathA.social: Hand-to-Chest. Place one hand on your forehead and one on your chest. Feel the 'container' of your own touch.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels loud, chaotic, and 'too much'. Goal: Narrowing the Focus.
pathB.body: Cup your hands around your eyes like goggles to limit your peripheral vision. Focus only on what is right in front of you.
pathB.mind: Focus on a single, repetitive sound in the room—a clock ticking or a fan humming.
pathB.social: Mirroring. Look at a safe person and try to match their slow, steady breathing pattern.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are drowning in your own internal sensations. Goal: Grounding the Pulse.

body.text: The Rhythmic Stomp. Stomp your feet firmly on the ground. Feel the impact travel through your bones. Left, Right, Left, Right.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-intensity bone conduction to provide an undeniable physical anchor when internal sensations feel 'borderless'.

mind.text: 5-4-3-2-1 Sensory Scan. Name 5 things you see, 4 you can touch, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to cycle through different sensory channels, which disrupts the 'panic loop' and re-engages the prefrontal cortex.

social.text: The Bi-Polar Containment. Place one hand firmly on your forehead and the other on your chest. Feel the 'container' of your own touch.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a physical sense of 'closure' and boundary to the upper torso and head during high-arousal flooding.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels loud, chaotic, and 'too much'. Goal: Narrowing the Focus.

body.text: Tunnel Vision Control. Cup your hands around your eyes like goggles to block peripheral vision. Focus only on one small spot in front of you.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the 'visual tax' on the brain by manually limiting incoming data, allowing the system to process one thing at a time.

mind.text: The Sound Anchor. Focus on a single, repetitive sound in the room—a clock ticking, a fan humming, or your own breath.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using a rhythmic, predictable auditory signal to synchronize a racing, chaotic internal tempo.

social.text: Co-Regulated Mirroring. Look at a safe person and try to match their slow, steady breathing pattern.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'mirror neurons' to outsource your regulation to a stable external nervous system.

pathB.title: Overload
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel overloaded. You have too many tabs open in your brain, and your system is beginning to 'glitch' under the pressure. You aren't failing; you are simply at maximum capacity.
pathB.blueprint: Cognitive Congestion. High sympathetic arousal is forcing your brain to try and solve 10 problems at once, leading to a decrease in executive function and an increase in agitation.
pathB.physicalSensation: Tightness across the forehead and temples; shallow breathing in the upper chest; and a 'buzzing' feeling in the brain that makes it hard to focus.
pathB.metaphor: A switchboard where every single light is blinking at once and the wires are starting to smoke.
pathB.theImpulse: To snap at anyone who speaks to you, to work faster and faster despite making mistakes, and to feel 'done' with everything.
pathB.superpower: High-Speed Processing. Your brain is trying to be a super-computer. It is attempting to protect your future by solving every possible problem right now.
pathB.generalGoal: Slowing the CPU. One thing at a time.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your mind is a racing loop of 'must-dos'. Goal: Externalizing the List.
pathA.body: Stand up and shake your arms and legs vigorously for 30 seconds. Imagine shaking the 'stress' off your skin.
pathA.mind: Brain Dump. Write down the 3 most urgent things on a piece of paper, then put the pen down.
pathA.social: Declare the Limit. Tell a safe person: 'I am at my limit. I need 10 minutes of silence to process.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like a checklist of demands. Goal: Creating a Sanctuary.
pathB.body: Put on noise-canceling headphones or earplugs to shut out the auditory 'noise'.
pathB.mind: Focus entirely on the feeling of your feet inside your shoes for one minute.
pathB.social: Ask for a 'No-Ask Zone'. Tell those around you that you cannot answer any questions for the next hour.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your mind is a racing loop of 'must-dos'. Goal: Externalizing the List.

body.text: The Tremor Discharge. Stand up and shake your arms and legs vigorously for 30 seconds. Imagine shaking the 'static' off your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using neurogenic-style shaking to discharge excessive sympathetic charge and reset muscle spindles.

mind.text: The Top 3 Triage. Write down exactly 3 urgent tasks on a scrap of paper, then physically put the pen in another room.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to prioritize and stop the 'infinite loop' of cognitive processing.

social.text: Declare the Limit. Tell a safe person: 'I am at my limit. I need 10 minutes of silence to process.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using verbal boundary setting to reduce the 'social tax' while in high-arousal overload.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like a checklist of demands. Goal: Creating a Sanctuary.

body.text: Auditory Shielding. Put on noise-canceling headphones or earplugs to shut out the environmental 'noise'.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the total amount of incoming data to allow the brain's 'switchboard' to cool down.

mind.text: The Foot-to-Floor Anchor. Focus entirely on the sensation of your feet inside your shoes and the pressure of the floor for one minute.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Moving focus from the high-speed 'head' energy to the stable, low-speed 'base' of the body.

social.text: The Question Embargo. Explicitly tell those around you: 'I am on a no-ask zone for the next hour. No questions unless it's an emergency.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Proactively stopping incoming social demands to prevent a high-arousal 'snap' or meltdown.

pathB.title: Twitchy
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel twitchy. You have an enormous amount of 'mobilization' energy ready to go, but nowhere to point it. You are like a racehorse behind the gate.
pathB.blueprint: Unchanneled Sympathetic Energy. Your body has dumped adrenaline into your system to prepare for action, but since you aren't fighting or fleeing, the energy is vibrating in your muscles.
pathB.physicalSensation: Restless legs; bouncing knees; tapping fingers; and a feeling of 'electricity' running under the skin.
pathB.metaphor: A live wire that has fallen on the ground—sparking and jumping with energy that has no grounded destination.
pathB.theImpulse: To pace the room, to bite your nails, to check your phone every 5 seconds, and to move without purpose.
pathB.superpower: Ready-For-Action. You are primed. If a real emergency happened right now, you would be the first person to move.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'buzz' is making you feel anxious internally. Goal: Directing the Spark.
pathA.body: Do 20 jumping jacks or a quick set of push-ups. Give that 'twitch' a job to do.
pathA.mind: Count the rhythm of your own tapping. 1-2-3-4, 1-2-3-4.
pathA.social: Hum a fast, rhythmic tune. Let the vibration of the humming match the vibration in your body.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too slow or annoying. Goal: Synchronizing with the room.
pathB.body: Find a physical rhythm in the environment (like a clock ticking) and try to match your tapping to it, then gradually slow it down.
pathB.mind: Focus on a single, repetitive visual task, like sorting coins or arranging pens.
pathB.social: Text a friend a funny, high-energy GIF to 'send' some of that jittery energy away.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'buzz' is making you feel anxious internally. Goal: Directing the Spark.

body.text: The Burst Discharge. Perform 20 jumping jacks or a quick set of push-ups. Give that 'twitch' a high-intensity job to do.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing a rapid metabolic 'sink' for excess adrenaline, utilizing large muscle groups to burn off the sympathetic surge.

mind.text: Rhythmic Counting. Tap a steady beat on your lap and count out loud: 1-2-3-4, 1-2-3-4.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using the prefrontal cortex to impose a structure on chaotic motor energy, shifting from 'twitch' to 'intentional rhythm'.

social.text: The Percussive Hum. Hum a fast, rhythmic tune with enough volume to feel your chest vibrate. Let it match your internal speed.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using vocal cord vibration to stimulate the auricular branch of the vagus nerve while venting motor energy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too slow or annoying. Goal: Synchronizing with the room.

body.text: Entrainment Braking. Match your finger tapping to an external rhythm (like a clock), then gradually and consciously slow the tap down over 30 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'entrainment' to manually down-shift the nervous system's internal clock by anchoring it to an external pace.

mind.text: Micro-Sorting. Focus on a single, repetitive manual task: sort coins by size, arrange pens by color, or stack some cards.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Channeling 'jittery' fine motor energy into an analytical, low-stakes task to re-engage the 'Task Positive Network'.

social.text: The GIF Dispatch. Text a friend a high-energy or funny GIF. Use the digital interaction to 'export' the jittery spark.
social.physioTip: Best for: Converting unchanneled physical energy into a brief social 'ping,' reducing the internal pressure of the 'Live Wire'.

pathB.title: Surging
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel a surge. Something unexpected just happened, and your system has 'spiked' its energy to meet the new information. You are in a state of high-alert appraisal.
pathB.blueprint: Acute Orientation Response. Your system is rapidly shifting gears to assess if a new stimulus is a threat or an opportunity, resulting in a sudden sympathetic surge.
pathB.physicalSensation: A sudden 'gasp' or sharp inhale; wide eyes; a cold chill running down the spine; and a momentary 'freeze' followed by a racing heart.
pathB.metaphor: A power surge in a building during a lightning storm—the lights flicker brightly for a second before the breakers reset.
pathB.theImpulse: To gasp, to stare, to ask 'What was that?', and to feel a sudden prickle of cold or heat.
pathB.superpower: Instant Adaptation. Your system just gave you a massive boost of awareness so you can survive the next 5 seconds, no matter what happens.
pathB.generalGoal: Stabilizing the current. Slowing the return.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'spike' feels overwhelming. Goal: Slow Exhale.
pathA.body: The Long Exhale. Inhale for 3 seconds, then exhale through pursed lips for a count of 8. Repeat 3 times.
pathA.mind: Name the Fact. Say out loud: 'That was a surprise, and I am safe right now.'
pathA.social: The Hand-on-Belly. Place your hand on your stomach and wait until you can feel it move with your breath again.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels startling or sharp. Goal: Re-orienting to Safety.
pathB.body: Look slowly to the left and slowly to the right. Take in the full 180° view of your surroundings.
pathB.mind: Identify 3 things in the room that have NOT changed in the last minute.
pathB.social: Reach out and touch a solid object (a desk, a wall). Feel its coldness and stability.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'spike' feels overwhelming. Goal: Slow Exhale.

body.text: The Resistance Exhale. Inhale for 3 seconds, then exhale through pursed lips (like blowing through a straw) for a count of 8.
body.physioTip: Best for: Creating positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) to mechanically stimulate the vagus nerve and slow the heart rate spike.

mind.text: The Fact Check. Say out loud: 'That was a surprise, and I am safe right now.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using language to override the survival brain's alarm and re-orient the prefrontal cortex to current safety.

social.text: Diaphragm Anchor. Place your hand on your belly and wait until you can feel it move with your breath again.
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing a connection with the 'midline' of the body after a shocking stimulus has 'ejected' focus to the periphery.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels startling or sharp. Goal: Re-orienting to Safety.

body.text: The Panoramic Scan. Look slowly to the far left and then slowly to the far right. Take in the full 180-degree view.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating the neck's proprioceptors and the vestibular system to signal the brain that the environment is clear of immediate threats.

mind.text: Constant Hunt. Identify 3 things in the room that have NOT changed in the last minute (e.g., the floor, the window, the chair).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a 'temporal anchor' to stabilize a brain that feels like it’s been hit by a sudden change.

social.text: The Solid Tether. Reach out and touch a solid, immovable object (a desk, a wall). Notice the temperature and the resistance.
social.physioTip: Best for: Outsourcing your sense of 'solidness' to the physical environment when your internal foundation feels shaky.

pathB.title: Burning
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are burning. Your system is radiating high-voltage energy because it's trying to incinerate a perceived threat or injustice. You aren't 'toxic'; you are protective.
pathB.blueprint: Thermal sympathetic activation. Your metabolic rate has spiked to generate heat and power, preparing your body for a high-intensity defense.
pathB.physicalSensation: Internal heat rising toward the face; dry mouth; a prickling sensation in the skin; and eyes that feel sharp or 'stinging'.
pathB.metaphor: A furnace with the door wide open. The heat is useful for forging, but it consumes everything if it's not directed.
pathB.theImpulse: To scorch others with words, to move fast and hard, and to find an immediate outlet for the internal fire.
pathB.superpower: Pure Vitality. You have the energy of a star. When cooled slightly, this is the power of absolute conviction and boundary setting.

pathA.body: The Sitali Breath. Curl your tongue and inhale through it like a straw (or inhale through pursed lips). Feel the cool air on your tongue.

mind.text: The Blue Mist. Visualize a blue, cool mist entering your lungs with every inhale, neutralizing the heat in your chest.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using color-association imagery to prime the nervous system for down-regulation and thermal settling.

mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'visual-thermal' cues to shift the brain's focus away from 'hot' emotional triggers.

social.physioTip: Best for: Using a change in environmental temperature to force the body's homeostatic sensors to pivot toward cooling.

pathB.metaphor: A pot on the stove with the lid rattling. The energy is contained, but only just barely.
pathB.theImpulse: To boil over, to snap, to hiss, and to release the pressure through an explosive outburst.
pathB.superpower: Transformative Force. Steam moves engines. This boiling energy is the precursor to massive change if the 'steam' is channeled through a pipe.

pathB.title: Clenched
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you are clenched. Your system has hardened your exterior to protect your vulnerability. You are a fortress under siege.
pathB.blueprint: Protective Armoring. High sympathetic arousal is causing sustained muscular contraction (bracing) to protect your joints and organs from impact.

pathB.theImpulse: To hold tighter, to refuse help, to grit your teeth, and to muscle through the pain.
pathB.superpower: Unshakeable Strength. In this state, you are an anchor. Nothing can move you or break you while you are braced.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside

pathA.social: Visualize yourself 'un-armoring' in a safe room, piece by piece.

mind.text: High-Definition Description. Pick one object and describe its color/texture in extreme detail: 'It is a matte, dusty navy with a slight metallic sheen.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to switch from 'scanning' (fast) to 'inspecting' (slow), clearing the cognitive fog.

pathB.blueprint: Sympathetic Hyper-reactivity. Your nervous system is primed for a 'snap' response. The brain is scanning for any further threat to justify an immediate explosion.

pathB.validation: It makes sense that you are trembling. This is your body's natural way of trying to 'shake off' or discharge trapped survival energy. It’s a sign that your system is trying to return to balance.
pathB.blueprint: Neurogenic Tremoring. Your sympathetic system is attempting to release the high-voltage charge of a 'fright' response. It is a biological 'reset' button.

social.text: The Vocal Hiss. Take a deep breath and exhale through your teeth with a long, steady 'Ssssss' sound until empty.
