body.text: The 50% Load. Place your hands flat on a wall and push with 50% of your strength. Feel the energy travel from your chest into the wall.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using sub-maximal isometric resistance to 'drain' excess motor energy into an immovable object, creating a sense of boundary.

mind.text: The Horizon Scan. Look as far away as possible—out a window or down a long hallway—to expand your sense of spatial boundaries.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'tunnel vision' of anger by expanding the visual field, which sends a safety signal to the superior colliculus.

social.text: The Boundary Declaration. State out loud: 'I have a lot of energy right now. I am going to take a 5-minute walk to vent.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Exercising social agency to create physical distance before the 'pressure' leads to a reactive snap.

pathB.title: Vibrating
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are vibrating. Your system is 'idling' at a high RPM, ready to move but waiting for a clear direction.
pathB.blueprint: Preparatory Sympathetic Arousal. Your motor neurons are firing at a high frequency to prepare your muscles for action, even if you are standing still.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'humming' or 'buzzing' feeling in the hands and feet; a butterfly sensation in the stomach; and an inability to sit still.
pathB.metaphor: A beehive in the summer—constant, high-frequency movement that sounds like a single, steady note.
pathB.theImpulse: To fidget, to check your phone repeatedly, and to talk faster than usual.
pathB.superpower: Alert Readiness. You are highly 'online'. Your reaction time is currently at its peak.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'hum' feels anxious internally. Goal: Rhythmic Grounding.
pathA.body: The Butterfly Hug. Cross your arms over your chest and slowly tap your shoulders, alternating left and right, like a slow metronome.
pathA.mind: The Internal Hum. Hum a song with a very steady, slow beat. Feel the vibration in your throat.
pathA.social: Text a friend a 'bee' emoji 🐝. A quick acknowledgement of your 'buzzing' energy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels a bit blurry or too fast. Goal: Visual Anchoring.
pathB.body: Find a physical rhythm in the room (a clock ticking, a fan spinning) and tap your finger to it.
pathB.mind: Pick one object and describe its texture in your head using 5 different words.
pathB.social: Ask a safe person to tell you a short, boring story about their day. Focus on the tone of their voice.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'hum' feels anxious internally. Goal: Rhythmic Grounding.

body.text: The Butterfly Hug. Cross your arms over your chest and slowly tap your shoulders, alternating left and right, like a slow metronome.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using bilateral alternating stimulation to organize high-frequency sympathetic 'noise' into a coherent, rhythmic signal.

mind.text: The Internal Hum. Hum a steady, low note. Focus on the physical vibration in your throat and chest rather than the sound.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the laryngeal branch of the vagus nerve to provide an internal 'anchor' for the vibrating sensation.

social.text: The Buzz Ping. Text a friend a 'bee' emoji 🐝. A quick, low-tax acknowledgement of your current 'buzzing' energy.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing the internal state to reduce the 'social masking' tax, which often adds fuel to nervous vibration.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels a bit blurry or too fast. Goal: Visual Anchoring.

body.text: External Entrainment. Find a physical rhythm in the room (clock, fan) and tap your finger to it. Once synchronized, gradually slow your tap.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'motor entrainment' to manually down-shift the nervous system's internal frequency by tethering it to a slower external pace.

mind.text: Texture Labeling. Pick one object and describe its texture in your head using 5 specific sensory words (e.g., grain, grit, cool, weave, slick).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the associative cortex to shift from high-speed 'scanning' to slow-speed 'evaluation,' clearing the nervous blur.

social.text: Prosody Focus. Ask a safe person to tell a boring story. Focus entirely on the melodic 'rise and fall' of their voice rather than the words.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing vocal prosody to signal the social engagement system that the environment is safe, lowering systemic arousal.

physio secret.title: Fidgety
physio secret.validation: It makes sense that you feel fidgety. You have a surplus of 'mobilization' energy and your system is trying to find a productive way to use it.
physio secret.blueprint: Low-level Sympathetic Discharge. Your body is using 'micro-movements' (fidgeting) to bleed off excess adrenaline and keep the system from becoming overloaded.
physio secret.physicalSensation: Bouncing legs; tapping fingers; playing with hair or jewelry; and a constant need to shift your posture.
physio secret.metaphor: A puppy waiting by the door with a leash in its mouth. Ready to go, eager, and full of undirected movement.
physio secret.theImpulse: To start three different tasks at once, to clean something, or to pace while thinking.
physio secret.superpower: Active Creativity. This restlessness is the 'itch' that leads to discovery and getting things done.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'itch' to move. Goal: Purposeful Motion.
pathA.body: The 10-Second Shake. Shake your hands and feet vigorously for 10 seconds, then stop and feel the 'tingle'.
pathA.mind: The List-Maker. Write down 3 tiny tasks you can do in under 2 minutes (e.g., 'water the plant').
pathA.social: Do a quick 'micro-task' for someone else (e.g., 'put their cup in the sink').

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Everything feels like a distraction. Goal: Tactile Focus.
pathB.body: Find a 'fidget' object—a pen to click, a stone to rub, or a piece of paper to fold.
pathB.mind: Focus on the feeling of your clothes against your skin as you move. Is it soft? Rough?
pathB.social: Play a quick 'game' with someone nearby, like 'I Spy' or a thumb-war.

physio secret.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'itch' to move. Goal: Purposeful Motion.

body.text: The Extremity Shake. Shake your hands and feet vigorously for 10 seconds, then stop abruptly and feel the 'tingle' of the blood return.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging minor sympathetic build-up by stimulating the distal mechanoreceptors and resetting the muscle spindles.

mind.text: The 2-Minute Triage. Identify 3 tiny tasks you can do in under 2 minutes (e.g., water a plant, clear a dish) and do ONE now.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Channeling restless motor energy into a 'completion loop,' which provides a small dopamine hit and settles the system.

social.text: The Micro-Service. Complete one tiny physical task that benefits your environment or someone else (e.g., tidying a shared space).
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'tend-and-befriend' circuit to shift away from jittery sympathetic arousal toward social regulation.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Everything feels like a distraction. Goal: Tactile Focus.

body.text: Object Manipulation. Find a 'fidget' object—a pen to click, a stone to rub, or fabric to fold. Focus on the resistance of the object.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing a continuous sensory-motor loop that occupies the 'excess' motor drive, allowing the brain to focus on other tasks.

mind.text: The Texture Map. Focus on the feeling of your clothes against your skin as you move. Is the texture rough, smooth, or heavy?
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from outward-focused distraction to inward-focused tactile grounding to 'anchor' the restlessness.

social.text: The Resistance Game. If with someone, engage in a quick thumb-war or a brief 'palm-push' match. Feel the counter-pressure.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast physical resistance with another person to rapidly discharge restless energy through co-regulation.

pathB.title: Pounding
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel a pounding sensation. Your heart is increasing its tempo to prepare your muscles for action. Even if you aren't moving, your body is ready to protect you.
pathB.blueprint: Cardiovascular Activation. Your sympathetic nervous system has increased your heart rate and stroke volume, making the pulse feel more prominent in your chest, neck, or ears.
pathB.physicalSensation: A rhythmic thumping in the chest; a pulsing feeling in the temples or throat; and a sense of internal 'drums' beating faster than the environment.
pathB.metaphor: An engine idling at a high speed, ready for the race to start. The vibration is felt through the whole frame of the car.
pathB.theImpulse: To check your pulse, to breathe rapidly, and to look for an immediate way to 'quiet' the noise in your chest.
pathB.superpower: Circulatory Power. Your body is efficiently moving oxygen and fuel exactly where it needs to go. You are physically primed for high performance.
pathB.generalGoal: Steadying the rhythm. Externalizing the beat.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'thump' is making you feel more anxious. Goal: The Brake Breath.
pathA.body: Extended Exhale. Inhale for 4 seconds, then exhale through your mouth as if through a straw for 8 seconds. Repeat 5 times.
pathA.mind: Mental Counting. Count backward from 100 by 7s. This forces the brain to leave the emotional loop and engage logic.
pathA.social: Hand-to-Chest. Place a firm hand over your heart. Say: 'I hear you, heart. Thank you for the energy.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels too loud or fast for your heartbeat. Goal: Sensory Matching.
pathB.body: Tap a slow, steady beat on your thigh with your hand. Focus on matching the tap to the speed you *want* your heart to be.
pathB.mind: The Grounding Scan. Find 5 square objects in the room and trace their corners with your eyes.
pathB.social: Safe Rhythm. If with a safe person, listen to the steady rhythm of their voice or a slow piece of music.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal 'thump' is making you feel more anxious. Goal: The Brake Breath.

body.text: The Baroreceptor Reset. Inhale for 4 seconds, then exhale through pursed lips (like a straw) for a full 8 seconds. Repeat 5 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using PEEP (Positive End-Expiratory Pressure) to stimulate the baroreceptors and the vagus nerve, mechanically forcing a decrease in heart rate.

mind.text: Serial 7s. Count backward from 100 by subtracting 7 each time (100, 93, 86...). Focus entirely on the math.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to exit the 'limbic hijack' by engaging the prefrontal cortex in a high-load analytical task.

social.text: The Heart Acknowledgment. Place a firm hand over your heart. Say out loud: 'I hear you, heart. Thank you for the energy. We are safe.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'self-compassion' cues and vocalization to reduce the fear-response to internal sensations (interoceptive exposure).

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels too loud or fast for your heartbeat. Goal: Sensory Matching.

body.text: Rhythmic Entrainment. Tap a slow, steady beat on your thigh. Focus on the sensation of your hand hitting your leg, creating a new, slower rhythm.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'motor-pacing' to provide the brain with a competing, intentional rhythm that overrides the involuntary pounding of the heart.

mind.text: Square Tracing. Find 5 square or rectangular objects in the room and slowly trace their four corners with your eyes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the ocular muscles in a structured, geometric pattern to settle the visual field and reduce scanning-related anxiety.

social.text: Prosody Anchor. If with a safe person, focus entirely on the low, steady 'hum' or rhythm of their voice rather than their words.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing external co-regulation to synchronize your internal tempo with a stable social signal.

pathB.title: Guarded
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel guarded. Your system is protecting your softest parts because it's not yet sure if the current environment is fully safe.
pathB.blueprint: Defensive Armoring. A state of mild sympathetic arousal where the muscles of the core, jaw, and shoulders slightly contract to protect vital areas from potential impact.
pathB.physicalSensation: Shoulders slightly raised; jaw held tight; and a subtle 'bracing' in the belly. You feel like you are wearing an invisible suit of armor.
pathB.metaphor: A drawbridge that has been raised halfway. You aren't closed off, but you are carefully controlling who and what gets in.
pathB.theImpulse: To cross your arms, to weigh your words carefully, and to monitor the movements of others in the room.
pathB.superpower: Strategic Discernment. You are not being 'cold'; you are being wise. You are curating your vulnerability until safety is proven.
pathB.generalGoal: Testing the perimeter. Softening the edges.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel tight and 'walled in' internally. Goal: Expanding the Breath.
pathA.body: Rib Expansion. Place hands on your waist. Breathe in and try to push your hands out to the sides with your ribs.
pathA.mind: The 'Safe Haven' visualization. Imagine a place where you never have to be guarded. Breathe in the air of that place.
pathA.social: Self-Touch. Gently rub your own shoulders to give your body the 'all-clear' signal of safe touch.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like it's watching you. Goal: Safe Exploration.
pathB.body: Orienting. Slowly turn your head to the left and right, noticing 3 things that are not a threat (a plant, a chair, a window).
pathB.mind: Object Appreciation. Find one object you find beautiful or interesting and list 3 details about it.
pathB.social: The Smile Check. Look at a neutral object and give it a tiny 'half-smile' to see how your face feels.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel tight and 'walled in' internally. Goal: Expanding the Breath.

body.text: Lateral Costal Breathing. Place your hands on the sides of your ribcage. Inhale and try to push your hands out to the sides with your ribs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rib expansion to create internal space without the 'vulnerability' of a full belly breath, softening the intercostal tension.

mind.text: The Safe Haven. Imagine a specific physical place where you feel 100% safe. Visualize the colors and temperature of that place.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using mental architecture to provide the safety data that the current environment is failing to deliver.

social.text: The Protective Rub. Gently rub your own upper arms or shoulders. Focus on the warmth and the intentionality of the touch.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing 'self-soothing' tactile input to lower the guarding reflex in the trapezius and shoulders.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like it's watching you. Goal: Safe Exploration.

body.text: The 180-Degree Scan. Slowly turn your head to the far left and far right. Notice 3 neutral objects that are definitively 'not a threat'.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using cervical rotation to signal the superior colliculus that the periphery is safe, allowing the 'braced' posture to settle.

mind.text: Detail Appreciation. Find one object you find interesting. Silently list 3 precise details (e.g., 'the grain of the wood', 'the shadow on the wall').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'threat-scanning' (broad and fast) to 'detailed observation' (narrow and slow), which settles the amygdala.

social.text: The Half-Smile (Vagal Ping). Look at a neutral object and give it a tiny, almost invisible 'half-smile'. Notice how your jaw and eyes feel.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the facial feedback loop to trigger the ventral vagal system and 'un-clench' the social engagement muscles.

pathB.title: Scanning
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you are scanning. Your brain is trying to predict and prevent problems by looking for any small cue of trouble. Your 'radar' is just working overtime.
pathB.blueprint: Hyper-Vigilant Orienting. The nervous system has narrowed your visual and auditory focus to detect threats, often leading to 'tunnel vision' or an inability to rest the eyes.
pathB.physicalSensation: Eyes darting from object to object; a feeling of 'lightness' in the head; and a neck that feels slightly stiff from constant movement.
pathB.metaphor: A lighthouse beam swinging rapidly across the water, searching for a rock that might not even be there.
pathB.theImpulse: To check your phone, to look at the door, and to mentally rehearse 'what-if' scenarios over and over.
pathB.superpower: High-Speed Pattern Recognition. You are currently a genius at noticing details. Your system is protecting your future by analyzing the present.
pathB.generalGoal: Grounding the gaze. Broadening the view.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your thoughts are scanning for 'what-ifs'. Goal: Facts over Fiction.
pathA.body: Foot Press. Press your big toes firmly into the ground. Feel the solid fact of the floor.
pathA.mind: The 'Is it True?' Game. Take one worry and ask: 'Is there proof for this right this second?'
pathA.social: The Verbal Anchor. Say out loud: 'I am here, and I am currently sitting in a chair.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Your eyes can't stop moving around the room. Goal: Panoramic Softness.
pathB.body: Panoramic Vision. Without moving your head, try to see the far-left and far-right edges of the room at the same time.
pathB.mind: Color Sorting. Find 3 green things, 3 red things, and 3 yellow things. This gives the scanning a task.
pathB.social: Focus on a neutral point. Pick a door handle or a light switch and look at it for 10 steady seconds.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your thoughts are scanning for 'what-ifs'. Goal: Facts over Fiction.

body.text: Hallux Grounding. Press your big toes (hallux) firmly into the ground. Feel the solid, undeniable fact of the floor.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a distal, high-contrast pressure point to pull awareness away from the 'head-heavy' anxiety of scanning.

mind.text: The Evidence Check. Take one specific 'what-if' and ask: 'Is there physical proof for this in this room right now?'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'predictive coding' loop of the brain by demanding real-time sensory data over future-based simulations.

social.text: The Verbal Anchor. Say out loud: 'I am here, and I am currently [state your physical action, e.g., sitting in a chair].'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using your own voice to create an 'auditory boundary' that anchors you in the present moment.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Your eyes can't stop moving around the room. Goal: Panoramic Softness.

body.text: Panoramic Vision. Without moving your head, soften your gaze to see the far-left and far-right edges of the room at the same time.
body.physioTip: Best for: Switching from foveal vision (sympathetic) to peripheral vision (parasympathetic), which inhibits the startle response.

mind.text: Targeted Categorization. Find 3 green things, 3 red things, and 3 yellow things. Say their names silently.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Giving the 'scanning' reflex an analytical job to do, which re-engages the prefrontal cortex.

social.text: Neutral Fixation. Pick a neutral, non-human object (a door handle or light switch) and look at it steadily for 10 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Practicing 'visual stillness' to prove to the nervous system that you can stop the threat-scan without being harmed.

pathB.pathB: no lion is currently chasing me.

pathB.title: Flushed
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel flushed. Your system is sending blood to your skin and face as part of a temperature-regulating and emotional response. It’s a visible sign of your internal heat.
pathB.blueprint: Vasodilation. Sympathetic arousal causes the blood vessels near the surface of the skin to widen, often as a reaction to social stress or rising anger.
pathB.physicalSensation: Heat in the cheeks, ears, or chest; a 'tingly' or 'prickly' feeling in the face; and a sense that the room has suddenly become warmer.
pathB.metaphor: A boiler that has been turned up just one notch too high—not exploding, but the pipes are hot to the touch.
pathB.theImpulse: To fan yourself, to look away, to splash water on your face, and to hide the visible 'redness'.
pathB.superpower: Honest Expression. Your body is telling the truth. Being flushed is a powerful, non-verbal signal that a boundary has been touched.
pathB.generalGoal: Cooling the current. Soothing the surface.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the heat rising within. Goal: Thermal Release.
pathA.body: Sitali Breath. Curl your tongue (or purse lips) and inhale cool air through the opening. Feel the cold air on your tongue.
pathA.mind: Blue Imagery. Visualize a cool, calm pool of blue water. Imagine your face dipping into it.
pathA.social: Self-Soothe. Place the back of your cold hands against your warm cheeks.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too 'close' or irritating. Goal: Creating Distance.
pathB.body: Cold Water. Run cold water over your wrists for 30 seconds. This is a physiological 'reset' for the system.
pathB.mind: The Window View. Look out a window at the furthest point possible. Feel the space between you and the horizon.
pathB.social: Step back. Move your body back by 2 feet to give your 'personal space' more room to breathe.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the heat rising within. Goal: Thermal Release.

body.text: Sitali Cooling. Curl your tongue (or purse lips) and inhale slowly through the opening. Feel the cold air on your tongue; exhale through the nose.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing evaporative cooling on the tongue to send an immediate 'temperature drop' signal to the hypothalamus.

mind.text: Blue Submersion. Visualize a cool, calm pool of deep blue water. Imagine the heat leaving your face as you dip into it.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using color-thermal association to prime the nervous system for vasoconstriction and cooling.

social.text: Thermal Conductance. Place the backs of your hands (usually cooler) firmly against your warm cheeks. Feel the heat transfer.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing an immediate, self-regulated 'heat sink' to reduce the physical sensation of flushing and social exposure.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too 'close' or irritating. Goal: Creating Distance.

body.text: The Radial Sink. Run cold water over your wrists for 30 seconds. Focus entirely on the temperature shift in your arms.
body.physioTip: Best for: Cooling the blood at a high-circulation point (radial artery) to help lower systemic thermal arousal.

mind.text: The Horizon Anchor. Look out a window at the furthest point possible. Notice the vast amount of space between you and the horizon.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Expanding the visual field to break 'social-threat tunnel vision' and lower the heart rate.

social.text: The Buffer Step. Physically move your body back by 2 feet. Notice if the extra 'personal space' makes the heat feel less intense.
social.physioTip: Best for: Adjusting the personal-space perimeter to reduce the 'proximity alert' that often triggers social flushing.

pathB.title: Prickly
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel prickly. Your system is raising its 'quills' to keep people at a safe distance while you navigate a minor annoyance.
pathB.blueprint: Mild Defensive Reactivity. A state where the threshold for 'irritation' is lowered, making neutral sounds or comments feel like sharp pokes.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'zinging' feeling in the skin; an urge to itch or twitch; and a general feeling of being 'on edge' or easily poked.
pathB.metaphor: A cactus in a busy room. You are well-protected, but anyone who gets too close might get a little sting.
pathB.theImpulse: To make a sharp comment, to roll your eyes, to move away from touch, and to 'bristle' at questions.
pathB.superpower: Sensory Sensitivity. You are noticing exactly what is not working in the environment. This is the first step toward refinement and change.
pathB.generalGoal: Smoothing the quills. Softening the contact.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'sharp' and irritable inside. Goal: Smoothing the Flow.
pathA.body: The Hand-Slide. Use one hand to firmly and slowly stroke down the opposite arm, from shoulder to fingertips. Repeat 3 times.
pathA.mind: Label the Poke. Identify the one thing that started the prickliness. Say: 'That was the poke, this is my reaction.'
pathA.social: Text a friend a 'hedgehog' emoji 🦔 to signal your current 'spiky' mood.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive. Goal: Tactile Comfort.
pathB.body: Find a soft fabric (a scarf, a cushion) and notice how it feels against your palms.
pathB.mind: Audio Softening. Focus on the quietest, softest sound you can find in the room.
pathB.social: Safe Distance. Give yourself permission to say: 'I'm feeling a bit spiky today, I need a little more personal space.'

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'sharp' and irritable inside. Goal: Smoothing the Flow.

body.text: The Proprioceptive Sweep. Use one hand to firmly and slowly stroke down the opposite arm, from shoulder to fingertips. Repeat 3 times per side.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using deep pressure to override 'prickly' superficial sensory input, helping to reset the threshold for tactile irritation.

mind.text: Label the Poke. Identify the specific stimulus that started the prickliness. Say: '[X] was the poke, this is just my system reacting.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Differentiating between the external trigger and the internal sensation, which reduces the urge to react sharply to others.

social.text: The Hedgehog Signal. Text a friend a hedgehog emoji 🦔. A quick, low-stakes way to signal that you are feeling 'spiky' and need gentle handling.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing a playful social anchor to externalize the state, reducing the pressure to mask irritation.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive. Goal: Tactile Comfort.

body.text: High-Contrast Softness. Find a soft fabric (scarf, cushion) and press your palms into it. Focus entirely on the lack of 'edges' in the sensation.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing the brain with 'non-threatening' tactile data to counteract the feeling of environmental abrasiveness.

mind.text: Audio Softening. Close your eyes and find the quietest, softest, most distant sound in the room. Follow it for 10 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'startle-ready' hearing to 'selective' hearing, which helps settle the auditory startle reflex.

social.text: The Boundary Buffer. Give yourself permission to say: 'I’m feeling a bit prickly today, I’m going to take a little extra space to reset.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Proactively managing the social perimeter to prevent an accidental 'sting' during an annoyed state.

pathB.title: Agitated
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel agitated. Your system has detected something 'off' or repulsive in your environment and is providing a low-dose spark to help you push it away or create distance.
pathB.blueprint: Boundary Alert. A mild sympathetic rise designed to increase your discernment and prompt you to move away from a stimulus that feels biologically or emotionally 'wrong'.
pathB.physicalSensation: A slight 'itch' in the skin; a desire to wipe your hands; or a subtle tightening of the upper lip and nose.
pathB.metaphor: Static electricity on a sweater; it’s not a shock, but it’s constant, annoying, and makes you want to shake it off.
pathB.theImpulse: To move away, to change the subject, or to physically 'shake' your body to clear the sensation.
pathB.superpower: Biological Integrity. You have a very fine-tuned 'disgust' response that protects you from things that aren't good for your system.
pathB.generalGoal: Clearing the field. Changing the input.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'ick' or agitation. Goal: Physical Clearance.
pathA.body: The Hand Shake. Shake your hands vigorously as if trying to flick water off your fingertips for 15 seconds.
pathA.mind: Label the Boundary. Say silently: 'I don't like this, and that is a valid piece of information.'
pathA.social: Text an emoji that represents your exit (🏃, 🚪) to a safe person to acknowledge your boundary.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive or 'sticky'. Goal: Sensory Refresh.
pathB.body: Change your sensory environment. Walk into a different room, open a window, or put on a different texture (like a jacket).
pathB.mind: Focus on a neutral, pleasant scent if available, or imagine the smell of fresh rain.
pathB.social: The 'Excuse Me'. Practice saying a low-stakes boundary: 'I'm going to step out for a breath of air.'

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'ick' or agitation. Goal: Physical Clearance.

body.text: The Distal Flick. Shake your hands vigorously as if flicking water off your fingertips for 15 seconds. Feel the release in your wrists.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rapid rhythmic movement to discharge the 'skin-crawling' sensation associated with high-disgust agitation.

mind.text: Label the Boundary. Say silently: 'My system is rejecting this input, and that is a valid survival signal.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'feeling gross' to 'observing a boundary,' which re-engages the prefrontal cortex.

social.text: The Boundary Ping. Send a simple 'exit' emoji (🏃, 🚪) to a safe person to acknowledge your need for space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing a boundary with zero verbal load to prevent social friction while feeling repulsed.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive or 'sticky'. Goal: Sensory Refresh.

body.text: Sensory Reset. Physically change your environment: move to a different room, open a window, or change one layer of clothing.
body.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'sensory lock' of an abrasive environment by providing the brain with fresh, neutral tactile data.

mind.text: Olfactory Anchor. Seek out a neutral or pleasant scent (coffee, a lemon, fresh air). If none are available, imagine the smell of fresh rain.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the olfactory system’s direct link to the emotional brain to rapidly 'overwrite' a disgust response.

social.text: The Neutral Exit. Practice a low-stakes boundary statement: 'I’m going to step out for a breath of air.' No further explanation needed.
social.physioTip: Best for: Reclaiming social agency and physical safety by asserting a simple, non-negotiable movement through space.

pathB.title: Turbulent
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel turbulent. Your internal 'weather' is changing, and your system is trying to stay balanced while the waves get a little choppier.
pathB.blueprint: Mild Sympathetic Instability. Your nervous system is fluctuating between 'seeking safety' and 'preparing for trouble', creating an uneven internal rhythm.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'swirling' feeling in the stomach; uneven breath; and a sense that your internal center of gravity is shifting.
pathB.metaphor: An airplane flying through a light cloud bank; there are occasional bumps, but the wings are still steady.
pathB.theImpulse: To fix something, to seek reassurance, or to keep moving so you don't have to feel the 'swirl'.
pathB.superpower: Navigational Awareness. You are sensitive to subtle changes in the 'currents' around you, allowing you to adjust your course early.
pathB.generalGoal: Stabilizing the hull. Finding the level.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'unsteady' and troubled internally. Goal: Rhythmic Leveling.
pathA.body: Box Breathing. Inhale for 4, hold for 4, exhale for 4, hold for 4. Use the rhythm to override the turbulence.
pathA.mind: Identify the 'Storm'. What is the one thing making the waves choppy? Name it, then set it aside for 5 minutes.
pathA.social: The Hand-on-Belly. Place your hand on your solar plexus and feel the warmth. Say: 'Steady now.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels unpredictable. Goal: Visual Leveling.
pathB.body: Find a horizontal line in the room (the floor, a table edge) and follow it slowly with your eyes from left to right.
pathB.mind: The 'Fact' Check. List 3 things in the room that are completely still and unmoving.
pathB.social: Ask a safe person for a 'grounding word' or just to sit quietly with you for a moment.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'unsteady' and troubled internally. Goal: Rhythmic Leveling.

body.text: Box Breathing. Inhale for 4, hold for 4, exhale for 4, hold for 4. Focus entirely on the squares of the rhythm.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic apnea (holds) to stabilize the heart rate and provide a structured 'metronome' for internal turbulence.

mind.text: Storm Labeling. Identify the one thing making the 'waves' choppy. Name it silently, then visualize setting it on a shelf for 5 minutes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing cognitive 'drag' by isolating the primary stressor, allowing the prefrontal cortex to regain control of the narrative.

social.text: Solar Plexus Grounding. Place your hand firmly on your solar plexus (just below the ribs). Feel the warmth and say silently: 'Steady now.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing an external tactile anchor for the diaphragm, encouraging a lower center of gravity for the breath.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels unpredictable. Goal: Visual Leveling.

body.text: The Horizontal Anchor. Find a long horizontal line (the floor, a shelf). Follow it slowly with your eyes from left to right and back again.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the vestibulo-ocular reflex to re-establish a sense of a stable 'horizon,' which counteracts the internal feeling of unsteadiness.

mind.text: Stillness Search. List 3 things in your immediate environment that are completely still and physically unmoving.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing the brain with 'fixed-point' data to override the internal sensation of swirling or movement.

social.text: The Grounding Word. Ask a safe person for a single 'grounding word' or to simply sit in quiet, unmoving presence with you for one minute.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing social co-regulation to provide a 'stable post' to hold onto while the internal turbulence settles.

pathB.title: Charged
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel charged. You have 'plugged in' to a concern, and your system is providing the electricity needed to pay attention and solve it.
pathB.blueprint: Attentional Arousal. Your sympathetic system is providing a low-level 'spark' to keep your brain sharp and your body ready to act on a concern.
pathB.physicalSensation: A light 'buzzing' in the forehead; ears that feel extra sensitive to sound; and a subtle 'ready' posture.
pathB.metaphor: A battery that has just been placed on the charger; there is a steady flow of energy starting to build up.
pathB.theImpulse: To ask questions, to double-check details, and to stay 'on alert' until the concern is resolved.
pathB.superpower: Focused Vigilance. You are highly 'online' and capable of catching small errors or risks before they grow.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'charge' is making your thoughts race. Goal: Earthing the Energy.
pathA.body: Barefoot Grounding. If possible, take off your shoes and feel the texture of the floor. Really 'dig' your heels in.
pathA.mind: The 'Priority Filter'. If you have 5 concerns, pick only the most important one to think about for the next 10 minutes.
pathA.social: The Concern Share. Tell a safe person: 'I'm feeling a bit charged about [X]. I just wanted to say it out loud.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels full of 'potential' problems. Goal: Sensory Simplification.
pathB.body: Decrease the input. Dim the lights or put on soft music to lower the 'voltage' coming from the room.
pathB.mind: Focus on the weight of your body in the chair. Feel the downward pull of gravity.
pathB.social: Look at a photo of a neutral, calm landscape (mountains or a forest).

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'charge' is making your thoughts race. Goal: Earthing the Energy.

body.text: Heel-Strike Grounding. If possible, take off your shoes. Press your heels firmly into the floor as if you are trying to leave an imprint in the ground.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-pressure input at the base of the body to pull awareness away from the 'attentional buzz' in the head and neck.

mind.text: The Priority Filter. If you have multiple concerns, pick the most urgent one. Set a mental timer to only think about that ONE thing for 10 minutes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the 'processing tax' on the brain by collapsing multiple low-level alerts into a single, manageable stream.

social.text: The Concern Share. Tell a safe person: 'I am feeling a bit charged about [X].' Acknowledging it out loud acts as a pressure release.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'affect labeling' to move the concern from the amygdala to the language centers of the brain, reducing internal charge.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels full of 'potential' problems. Goal: Sensory Simplification.

body.text: Input Reduction. Lower the 'voltage' of the room: dim the lights, silence notifications, or step into a quieter corner.
body.physioTip: Best for: Manually lowering the amount of data the 'Charged' brain has to process, preventing a spike into higher arousal.

mind.text: Gravitational Loading. Focus entirely on the weight of your pelvis and thighs in the chair. Feel the downward pull of gravity supporting you.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'heavy' sensory data to counteract the 'light/buzzy' feeling of head-centered anxiety.

social.text: Visual Dilation. Find the largest neutral surface in the room (a blank wall or the floor). Let your eyes rest there for 20 seconds without 'looking' for anything.
social.physioTip: Best for: Switching off the 'target-lock' visual mode that fuels hyper-vigilance, allowing the visual system to settle.

pathB.title: Unsettled
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel unsettled. Your system is in a 'wait and see' mode, sensing that things aren't quite aligned, which creates a mild internal friction.
pathB.blueprint: Low-grade Orienting Tension. Your nervous system is scanning for a missing piece of safety or information, keeping you in a state of 'unguarded' readiness.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'shifting' feeling in the chest; a need to move your feet; and eyes that won't settle on one point for very long.
pathB.metaphor: Dust motes dancing in a beam of light—constantly moving, never quite landing, waiting for the air to go still.
pathB.theImpulse: To pace, to adjust your clothing, and to 'look around' for the source of the unease.
pathB.superpower: Intuitive Sensing. You are picking up on a subtle mismatch in your environment. Trust your system's ability to 'notice' the nuances.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'shiftiness'. Goal: Centering the Weight.
pathA.body: The Pelvic Drop. Sit firmly and imagine your sit-bones growing roots into the chair. Let your weight settle downward.
pathA.mind: The 'Right Now' List. Name 3 things that are definitely true in this exact second (e.g., 'I am breathing').
pathA.social: Self-Squeeze. Cross your arms and give your opposite elbows a firm, steady squeeze.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels 'off'. Goal: Physical Tethers.
pathB.body: Touch 3 different textures in the room—something hard, something soft, something cold.
pathB.mind: Find a single spot on the wall and look at it steadily for 20 seconds without moving your eyes.
pathB.social: Ask a safe person a 'grounding' question: 'What are you planning for dinner?' (Brings the focus to the mundane).

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'shiftiness'. Goal: Centering the Weight.

body.text: Ischial Tuberosity Grounding. Sit firmly on a hard surface if possible. Rock slightly until you feel the sharp 'sit-bones' in your pelvis, then settle your full weight directly onto them.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using skeletal loading to provide a clear 'vertical anchor' for a nervous system that feels internally shifty or untethered.

mind.text: The 'Right Now' List. Name 3 things that are biologically true in this exact second (e.g., 'My feet are touching the floor', 'I can feel the air in my nose').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Interrupting the 'future-threat' scanning by forcing the brain to process immediate, non-negotiable sensory data.

social.text: Bilateral Humerus Compression. Cross your arms and give your opposite upper arms (the humerus bone) a firm, steady squeeze. Hold for 10 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a 'boundary' signal to the brain, mimicking a hug to activate the ventral vagal system and reduce internal friction.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels 'off'. Goal: Physical Tethers.

body.text: Tactile Discriminatory Scan. Touch 3 different textures: something hard (desk), something soft (fabric), and something cold (metal). Focus on the distinct difference between them.
body.physioTip: Best for: Shifting the brain from 'distal threat scanning' (eyes/ears) to 'proximal safety testing' (hands), which lowers the arousal state.

mind.text: The 20-Second Fixation. Pick a single, small spot on the wall. Look at it steadily for 20 seconds without letting your eyes wander or dart.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Manually inhibiting the superior colliculus's urge to 'scan,' teaching the eyes that it is safe to remain still.

social.text: The Mundane Anchor. Ask a safe person a 'grounding' question: 'What’s on your schedule for tomorrow?' Focus on the practical, boring details of the answer.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing social entrainment to shift the conversation—and the brain—away from abstract unease toward concrete reality.

pathB.title: Tense
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel tense. You’ve encountered a small 'knot' in your day, and your muscles are tightening to help you pull through it.
pathB.blueprint: Mild Sympathetic Tone. A slight increase in muscle spindles' sensitivity, preparing your body to exert force if the 'peeved' feeling escalates.
pathB.physicalSensation: A tight jaw; a 'string' pulling between the shoulder blades; and a feeling of being 'wound up' like a spring.
pathB.metaphor: A guitar string that has been tuned just a half-note too sharp. The sound is clear, but it feels like it might snap if pulled harder.
pathB.theImpulse: To sigh with frustration, to move with 'sharp' gestures, and to be short with your answers.
pathB.superpower: Ready-to-Act. This tension is 'work-ready' energy. It is the beginning of the focus needed to solve a problem or set a boundary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the 'knot' internally. Goal: Direct Release.
pathA.body: The Shoulder Drop. Inhale and pull your shoulders to your ears, then exhale and let them 'drop' like weights.
pathA.mind: The 'Half-Smile'. Try to soften only the corners of your mouth. Notice if that changes the tension in your jaw.
pathA.social: Text a 'sigh' emoji 💨 to someone who understands your current frustration.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels annoying or high-pressure. Goal: Horizon Softening.
pathB.body: Look as far away as possible (out a window) and let your eyes go 'soft' and out of focus for 10 seconds.
pathB.mind: Find one object in the room that looks 'relaxed' (a draped blanket, a sleeping pet).
pathB.social: The 'Yawn' Hack. Even if you aren't tired, fake a large, wide yawn. This is a biological 'reset' for the jaw and throat.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the 'knot' internally. Goal: Direct Release.

body.text: The Scapular Drop. Inhale deeply while pulling your shoulders toward your ears, then exhale sharply and let the scapulae drop like heavy weights.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using brief, high-intensity contraction followed by a gravity-assisted release to reset the tone of the upper trapezius and levator scapulae.

mind.text: The Micro-Softening. Focus only on the corners of your mouth. Soften them by 10% and notice if your jaw follows suit.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the facial feedback loop to send a 'low-threat' signal to the brainstem, reducing secondary jaw tension.

social.text: The Sigh Signal. Text a 'sigh' emoji 💨 to a trusted friend. It acknowledges the 'peeved' state without requiring a complex social exchange.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing minor irritation to prevent it from 'stewing' into higher-intensity states like 'Boiling' or 'Fuming'.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels annoying or high-pressure. Goal: Horizon Softening.

body.text: The Foveal Break. Look at the furthest point possible (out a window). Soften your gaze until the edges of your vision blur for 10 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Inhibiting foveal (narrow) vision to shift the brain from a 'problem-solving' sympathetic state to a 'global-scanning' parasympathetic state.

mind.text: Relaxation Mirroring. Find one object in your environment that looks 'limp' or 'relaxed' (a draped cloth, a plant leaf, a sleeping pet).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using visual 'anchors' of low-tone objects to prime the brain for its own muscular down-regulation.

social.text: Pharyngeal Reset. Perform a wide, exaggerated 'fake' yawn. Stretch the back of your throat and open your mouth as wide as possible.
social.physioTip: Best for: Mechanically stretching the muscles of the jaw and pharynx to stimulate the glossopharyngeal nerve and trigger a relaxation response.

pathB.title: Still
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel still. Your system has reached a rare point of total equilibrium where no effort is required. You are safely anchored in the present moment.
pathB.blueprint: Ventral Vagal dominance. Your parasympathetic system is in its most restorative 'rest and digest' mode. Heart rate and breathing are at their most efficient, rhythmic baseline.
pathB.physicalSensation: A feeling of weightlessness or 'soft' boundaries; muscles are completely devoid of holding-tension; breath is deep, slow, and effortless.
pathB.metaphor: A mountain lake at dawn—the water is so flat it acts as a perfect mirror, undisturbed by even a single ripple.
pathB.theImpulse: To remain exactly as you are, to breathe deeply, and to simply 'be' without any need to do.
pathB.superpower: Deep Restoration. In this state of total stillness, your body is performing its most vital cellular and emotional repair work.
pathB.generalGoal: Savoring the sanctuary. Deepening the rest.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are deeply submerged in internal peace. Goal: Quiet Awareness.
pathA.body: Scan your body for the most 'quiet' spot—perhaps your earlobes or the tip of your nose. Just feel its stillness.
pathA.mind: Let your thoughts pass like distant clouds. Don't grab them; just watch them float by.
pathA.social: Visualize a circle of light around you, keeping this peace safe and undisturbed.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels distant and non-threatening. Goal: Gentle Inclusion.
pathB.body: Notice the feeling of the air on your skin. Is it cool? Warm? Soft?
pathB.mind: Identify one sound in the distance (a bird, the wind) and notice how it doesn't disturb your stillness.
pathB.social: Mentally send a 'thank you' to your environment for providing this moment of safety.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are deeply submerged in internal peace. Goal: Quiet Awareness.

body.text: Neutral Point Scanning. Scan your body for the most 'neutral' or quiet spot (e.g., your earlobes, the back of your knees). Simply rest your attention there.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reinforcing the brain's map of 'safety-points' in the body, which serves as a physiological anchor for future high-stress states.

mind.text: Cloud Watching. Allow thoughts to appear and pass like distant clouds. Observe them without 'grabbing' or analyzing them.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Practicing 'metacognitive awareness'—shifting from being the thinker to the observer of thoughts, which reduces cognitive load.

social.text: The Luminous Perimeter. Visualize a soft circle of light surrounding you, acting as a gentle, permeable boundary that keeps this peace secure.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using symbolic imagery to maintain the Ventral Vagal state in shared spaces without needing to physically isolate.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels distant and non-threatening. Goal: Gentle Inclusion.

body.text: Atmospheric Boundary Sensing. Notice the subtle feeling of the air moving against your skin. Observe the temperature and the weight of your clothing.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing low-threshold mechanoreceptors to confirm environmental safety, further deepening the 'rest and digest' response.

mind.text: Harmonic Orienting. Identify one distant, repetitive sound (wind, a clock). Notice how your internal stillness effortlessly absorbs the sound.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-engaging the auditory system in a non-threatening way, preventing the 'startle reflex' from being triggered by sudden changes.

social.text: Environmental Gratitude. Mentally or whisper a 'thank you' to your current surroundings for providing this window of absolute safety.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using gratitude to stimulate the prefrontal cortex's 'reward' pathways, chemically cementing the state of serenity.

pathB.title: Warm
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel warm. Your system has relaxed its 'constriction,' allowing blood and energy to flow freely to your skin and extremities. You are safe and held.
pathB.blueprint: Optimal Vasodilation. Your nervous system is signaling that the environment is hospitable, allowing the body to release its 'survival' grip and expand into a state of comfort.
pathB.physicalSensation: A pleasant 'glow' in the chest and belly; soft, warm hands and feet; and a feeling of 'melting' into your seat.
pathB.metaphor: Sunlight hitting a patch of moss in the forest; a slow, steady radiance that seeps into everything it touches.
pathB.theImpulse: To snuggle, to wrap yourself in something soft, and to let your eyes go heavy and soft.
pathB.superpower: Somatic Comfort. You are radiating safety. This warmth is the biological 'signal' that allows for deep social bonding and healing.
pathB.generalGoal: Nurturing the glow. Expanding the comfort.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a beautiful internal glow. Goal: Internal Radiance.
pathA.body: Place your hands on your belly and feel the warmth of your own palms passing through your clothes.
pathA.mind: Imagine this warmth as a golden liquid filling your body from your toes to your crown.
pathA.social: Recall a memory of a time you felt completely 'at home' with someone.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a beautiful internal glow. Goal: Internal Radiance.

body.text: Thermal Biofeedback. Place your palms firmly on your belly or chest. Focus entirely on the heat transfer from your core to your hands.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing cutaneous thermoreceptors to confirm the body's 'expansion' state, reinforcing the Ventral Vagal safety signal.

mind.text: Golden Flow. Imagine a warm, golden liquid slowly filling your body from the soles of your feet to the crown of your head.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'visceral imagery' to facilitate systemic relaxation and further lower any residual sympathetic tone.

social.text: The Belonging Recall. Recall a specific moment when you felt completely 'at home' or safe in the presence of another person.
social.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain's 'attachment circuitry' to deepen the release of oxytocin, which acts as a natural cardioregulator.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels inviting and soft. Goal: Tactile Savoring.

body.text: The Softness Scan. Find a soft texture (sweater, blanket, or skin) and move your fingertips over it as slowly as possible.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating C-tactile fibers, which are specifically tuned to slow, gentle touch and send 'pro-social' safety signals to the brain.

mind.text: Comfort Inventory. Identify 3 objects in the room that look 'comfortable' or 'hospitable'. Notice how their presence makes you feel.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Training the brain to 'seek comfort' rather than 'scan for threats,' shifting the baseline of environmental appraisal.

social.text: The Compassionate Wrap. Imagine yourself wrapping a heavy, warm blanket around a person you care about. See them relax as you do.
social.physioTip: Best for: Moving from 'self-safety' to 'co-regulation,' which strengthens the social engagement system (Ventral Vagal branch).

pathB.title: Quiet
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel quiet. Your internal 'noise' has settled, and your system is no longer scanning for problems. You are in a state of tranquil observation.
pathB.blueprint: Reduced Auditory Vigilance. Your nervous system has tuned out the 'high-frequency' scanning for danger, allowing you to hear the subtle, rhythmic sounds of life and safety.
pathB.physicalSensation: A lack of 'buzzing' in the head; relaxed ears; and a sense of space behind the forehead. The world feels 'muffled' in a pleasant, soft way.
pathB.metaphor: A library in the middle of a forest; the only sounds are the turning of pages and the rustle of leaves.
pathB.theImpulse: To whisper, to move slowly, and to listen to the silence between sounds.
pathB.superpower: Clarity of Mind. In this quiet, you can perceive the most subtle details and truths that are usually drowned out by the noise of survival.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a deep, hushed internal space. Goal: Inner Listening.
pathA.body: Close your eyes and listen to the sound of your own heartbeat or your own soft breath.
pathA.mind: Notice the space *between* your thoughts. How long is the silence there?
pathA.social: Enjoy the quiet 'company' of your own presence. Say silently: 'I am here.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside

pathB.mind: Focus on a single point on a wall and notice the 'quietness' of the color.
pathB.social: Look at a photo of a safe person and enjoy the 'silent conversation' you have with their image.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a deep, hushed internal space. Goal: Inner Listening.

body.text: Interoceptive Audition. Close your eyes and focus entirely on the sound of your own heartbeat or the soft 'whoosh' of your breath in your throat.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the 'midline' of the body, utilizing the rhythm of the heart to provide a biological anchor for peace.

mind.text: The Gap Search. Notice the space or the 'gap' between your thoughts. Observe how long that silence lasts before the next thought appears.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Practicing 'non-attachment' to cognitive noise, which helps maintain low sympathetic arousal and mental clarity.

social.text: The Silent 'I Am'. Enjoy the quiet company of your own presence. Whisper or say silently: 'I am here, and it is quiet.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using self-affirmation to cement the sense of safety, reinforcing the Ventral Vagal state of social engagement with the self.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels peaceful and still. Goal: Audio Savoring.

body.text: The Floor-Noise Scan. Listen for the quietest, most constant sound you can find (a distant hum, a clock, or the air moving). Focus on its steadiness.
body.physioTip: Best for: Training the auditory system to rest on neutral stimuli, which lowers the threshold for 'startle' responses.

mind.text: Visual Silence. Pick a neutral-colored surface. Observe the 'quietness' of the color and the lack of movement on the texture.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing visual-motor activity to match the auditory quietness, creating a total-system state of tranquility.

social.text: The Silent Proxy. Look at a photo of a safe person. Imagine the 'quiet comfort' of their presence without needing to exchange words.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing symbolic social connection to maintain a high-safety vital (+3) without the metabolic cost of active communication.

pathB.title: Grounded
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel grounded. Your system is fully supported by the environment, and you have 'dropped' your weight into that support.
pathB.blueprint: Optimal Proprioceptive Feedback. Your nervous system is receiving strong signals from your muscles and joints that you are stable, leading to a sense of physical 'heaviness' and emotional security.
pathB.physicalSensation: A strong awareness of your feet and seat; a feeling of being 'solid' and unshakeable; and a low center of gravity in the pelvis.
pathB.metaphor: An ancient oak tree with roots that go deep into the earth. The wind may blow the leaves, but the trunk is immovable.
pathB.theImpulse: To sit firmly, to push your heels into the floor, and to take up space with a sense of 'belonging'.
pathB.superpower: Unshakeable Stability. When you are grounded, you become a 'safe harbor' for others. You are the 'floor' that people can stand on.
pathB.generalGoal: Reinforcing the connection. Solidifying the base.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'solid' and heavy in a good way. Goal: Deep Anchoring.
pathA.body: Squeeze your thighs and feel the density of your muscles. Feel the 'meat on your bones'.
pathA.mind: Identify the 'lowest' point of your body right now. Focus all your attention there for 10 seconds.
pathA.social: Visualize 'roots' growing from your seat down into the very center of the earth.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The floor and furniture feel like allies. Goal: Testing the Support.
pathB.body: Shift your weight from side to side. Notice how the chair or floor holds you every single time.
pathB.mind: Find 3 vertical objects (walls, doorframes) and notice how they stand straight and strong.
pathB.social: Touch a solid surface—a table or a wall—and appreciate its 'un-moving' nature.

pathB.warning: N/A

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'solid' and heavy in a good way. Goal: Deep Anchoring.

body.text: Skeletal Density Sensing. Squeeze your thigh muscles firmly against the bone. Feel the density and 'weight' of your own legs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reinforcing the brain's cortical map of the lower limbs, which lowers the center of gravity and increases the sense of physical 'presence'.

mind.text: The Gravitational Plumb Line. Identify the lowest point of your body in contact with the ground. Focus all your attention there, feeling the downward pull of gravity.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using the constant of gravity as a 'biological anchor' to settle any remaining cognitive 'chatter' or high-frequency scanning.

social.text: The Deep Root. Visualize your sit-bones growing heavy, deep roots into the center of the earth, making you part of the landscape.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using symbolic 'belonging' to ground the nervous system in the immediate environment, reducing the need for social 'masking'.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The floor and furniture feel like allies. Goal: Testing the Support.

body.text: The Weight Shift. Slowly rock your weight from your left sit-bone to your right. Notice how the chair provides 100% resistance and support every time.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'closed-chain' movement to prove to the vestibular system that the support structure is reliable and unmoving.

mind.text: Verticality Hunt. Find 3 vertical objects (walls, doorframes, pillars). Notice how they stand straight and carry their load without effort.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain with visual cues of 'structural integrity,' which naturally prompts the user's own posture to settle into a grounded state.

social.text: Structural Solidarity. Place your hand flat on a solid surface (a stone wall or heavy table). Silently acknowledge its 'un-moving' nature and your connection to it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing the sense of being 'solid' by anchoring to an immovable object, creating a shared sense of stability.

pathB.title: Warm
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel warm. Your system has relaxed its 'constriction,' allowing blood and energy to flow freely to your skin and extremities. You are safe and held.
pathB.blueprint: Optimal Vasodilation. Your nervous system is signaling that the environment is hospitable, allowing the body to release its 'survival' grip and expand into a state of comfort.
pathB.physicalSensation: A pleasant 'glow' in the chest and belly; soft, warm hands and feet; and a feeling of 'melting' into your seat.
pathB.metaphor: Sunlight hitting a patch of moss in the forest; a slow, steady radiance that seeps into everything it touches.
pathB.theImpulse: To snuggle, to wrap yourself in something soft, and to let your eyes go heavy and soft.
pathB.superpower: Somatic Comfort. You are radiating safety. This warmth is the biological 'signal' that allows for deep social bonding and healing.
pathB.generalGoal: Nurturing the glow. Expanding the comfort.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a beautiful internal glow. Goal: Internal Radiance.

body.text: Thermal Biofeedback. Place your palms firmly on your belly or chest. Focus entirely on the heat transfer from your core to your hands.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing cutaneous thermoreceptors to confirm the body's 'expansion' state, reinforcing the Ventral Vagal safety signal.

mind.text: Golden Flow. Imagine a warm, golden liquid slowly filling your body from the soles of your feet to the crown of your head.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'visceral imagery' to facilitate systemic relaxation and further lower any residual sympathetic tone.

social.text: The Belonging Recall. Recall a specific moment when you felt completely 'at home' or safe in the presence of another person.
social.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain's 'attachment circuitry' to deepen the release of oxytocin, which acts as a natural cardioregulator.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels inviting and soft. Goal: Tactile Savoring.

body.text: The Softness Scan. Find a soft texture (sweater, blanket, or skin) and move your fingertips over it as slowly as possible.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating C-tactile fibers, which are specifically tuned to slow, gentle touch and send 'pro-social' safety signals to the brain.

mind.text: Comfort Inventory. Identify 3 objects in the room that look 'comfortable' or 'hospitable'. Notice how their presence makes you feel.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Training the brain to 'seek comfort' rather than 'scan for threats,' shifting the baseline of environmental appraisal.

social.text: The Compassionate Wrap. Imagine yourself wrapping a heavy, warm blanket around a person you care about. See them relax as you do.
social.physioTip: Best for: Moving from 'self-safety' to 'co-regulation,' which strengthens the social engagement system (Ventral Vagal branch).

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Grounded
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel grounded. Your system is fully supported by the environment, and you have 'dropped' your weight into that support.
pathB.blueprint: Optimal Proprioceptive Feedback. Your nervous system is receiving strong signals from your muscles and joints that you are stable, leading to a sense of physical 'heaviness' and emotional security.
pathB.physicalSensation: A strong awareness of your feet and seat; a feeling of being 'solid' and unshakeable; and a low center of gravity in the pelvis.
pathB.metaphor: An ancient oak tree with roots that go deep into the earth. The wind may blow the leaves, but the trunk is immovable.
pathB.theImpulse: To sit firmly, to push your heels into the floor, and to take up space with a sense of 'belonging'.
pathB.superpower: Unshakeable Stability. When you are grounded, you become a 'safe harbor' for others. You are the 'floor' that people can stand on.
pathB.generalGoal: Reinforcing the connection. Solidifying the base.
