pathA.goal: You feel 'sharp' and irritable inside. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: High-Contrast Softness. Find a soft fabric nearby (a scarf, a cushion, or even a soft part of your own clothing). Press your palms into it slowly and notice the total lack of 'edges' or sharpness.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing the brain with 'non-threatening' external tactile data to counteract the internal feeling of being 'spiky', pulling awareness into a softer environment.

mind.text: Audio Softening. Close your eyes. Search for the quietest, softest, most distant sound in the room (e.g., a distant bird or a faint hum). Follow that specific sound for 15 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'startle-ready' scanning to 'selective' soft hearing, which helps settle the auditory startle reflex and anchors focus outside the irritated self.

social.text: The Boundary Buffer. State out loud or mentally: 'I am feeling a bit prickly right now, so I am going to expand my personal space by two feet.' Physically move slightly away from others.
social.physioTip: Best for: Proactively managing the social perimeter to prove to the nervous system that you have the agency to protect your own boundaries without needing to 'sting'.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive and 'poky'. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Proprioceptive Sweep. Use your right hand to firmly and slowly stroke down your left arm, from shoulder to fingertips. Feel the heavy pressure. Repeat 3 times, then switch sides.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using deep pressure (A-beta fiber input) to override the 'prickly' superficial sensory signals, pulling awareness back into the somatic container to reset the irritation threshold.

mind.text: Label the Poke. Identify the exact stimulus that triggered the prickliness. Say silently: '[X] was the poke. That was out there, but this is just my system reacting in here.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Differentiating between the external trigger and the internal sensation, which re-engages the prefrontal cortex and anchors you back in your own 'center'.

social.text: The Hedgehog Signal. Text a safe person a hedgehog emoji 🦔. If alone, look at your own palms and say: 'I’m feeling spiky, and that’s okay. I’m staying inside my shell for a moment.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing a playful social anchor to inhabit the state, reducing the metabolic drain of 'social masking' and reclaiming your internal safety.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Agitated
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel agitated. Your system has detected something 'off' or repulsive in your environment and is providing a low-dose spark to help you push it away or create distance.
pathB.blueprint: Boundary Alert. A mild sympathetic rise designed to increase your discernment and prompt you to move away from a stimulus that feels biologically or emotionally 'wrong'.
pathB.physicalSensation: A slight 'itch' in the skin; a desire to wipe your hands; or a subtle tightening of the upper lip and nose.
pathB.metaphor: Static electricity on a sweater; it’s not a shock, but it’s constant, annoying, and makes you want to shake it off.
pathB.theImpulse: To move away, to change the subject, or to physically 'shake' your body to clear the sensation.
pathB.superpower: Biological Integrity. You have a very fine-tuned 'disgust' response that protects you from things that aren't good for your system.
pathB.generalGoal: Clearing the field. Changing the input.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'ick' or agitation. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Sensory Reset. Physically change your immediate environment. Move to a different room, step onto a different floor texture, or open a window to change the air flow.
body.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'sensory lock' of an abrasive environment by providing the brain with fresh, neutral external data to overwrite the internal agitation.

mind.text: Olfactory Anchor. Seek out a strong, neutral or pleasant scent (e.g., coffee beans, a cut lemon, or fresh peppermint). Focus entirely on the inhale.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the olfactory bulb's direct pathway to the limbic system to rapidly 're-code' a disgust response, pulling focus into the room.

social.text: The Neutral Exit. Practice a low-stakes boundary statement out loud: 'I’m going to step out for a minute for some fresh air.' Then physically execute the move.
social.physioTip: Best for: Reclaiming social agency by asserting a simple, non-negotiable movement through space, proving to your system that you can exit the 'repulsive' zone.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive or 'sticky'. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Distal Flick. Shake your hands vigorously as if flicking water off your fingertips for 15 seconds. Then, press your palms together firmly and feel the stillness.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rapid rhythmic movement to discharge the 'skin-crawling' sensation, followed by isometric pressure to pull awareness back to the midline.

mind.text: Label the Boundary. Identify the sensation and say silently: 'My system is detecting an mismatch. This feeling is my body protecting my borders. I am in control of my skin.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'feeling gross' to 'observing a biological signal,' which re-engages the prefrontal cortex and anchors you back in your center.

social.text: The Boundary Ping. Send a simple 'exit' or 'shield' emoji to a safe person. If alone, look at your own hands and acknowledge: 'Safety is inside here; the mess is out there.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing a boundary with zero verbal load, which protects the social engagement system while you re-establish your internal 'container'.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Turbulent
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel turbulent. Your internal 'weather' is changing, and your system is trying to stay balanced while the waves get a little choppier.
pathB.blueprint: Mild Sympathetic Instability. Your nervous system is fluctuating between 'seeking safety' and 'preparing for trouble', creating an uneven internal rhythm.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'swirling' feeling in the stomach; uneven breath; and a sense that your internal center of gravity is shifting.
pathB.metaphor: An airplane flying through a light cloud bank; there are occasional bumps, but the wings are still steady.
pathB.theImpulse: To fix something, to seek reassurance, or to keep moving so you don't have to feel the 'swirl'.
pathB.superpower: Navigational Awareness. You are sensitive to subtle changes in the 'currents' around you, allowing you to adjust your course early.
pathB.generalGoal: Stabilizing the hull. Finding the level.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'unsteady' and troubled internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Horizontal Anchor. Find a long, steady horizontal line in the room (the floor, a shelf, the horizon). Follow it slowly with your eyes from left to right and back again 3 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the vestibulo-ocular reflex to re-establish a sense of a stable 'horizon' in the brain, pulling focus out of the internal sensation of unsteadiness.

mind.text: Stillness Search. Identify 3 things in your immediate environment that are completely still and physically unmoving (e.g., a heavy table, a wall, a lamp).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing the brain with 'fixed-point' data to override the internal sensation of swirling, anchoring the mind in objective external stability.

social.text: The Grounding Word. If with someone, ask for a single 'grounding' word. If alone, look at a solid object and say a word that represents 'stillness' out loud.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing an external auditory signal or co-regulation to provide a 'stable post' to hold onto, pulling the focus away from the internal turbulence.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels unpredictable. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Box Breathing. Inhale for 4, hold for 4, exhale for 4, hold for 4. Focus entirely on the physical sensation of the air and the 'hold' at the bottom.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic holds to stabilize the heart rate and provide a structured internal 'metronome' to pull the spaced-out brain back into the body.

mind.text: Storm Labeling. Identify the one specific thing making the internal waves choppy. Name it silently, then visualize setting it on a shelf inside your mind for 5 minutes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing cognitive 'drag' by isolating the primary stressor, allowing the prefrontal cortex to regain control of the internal state.

social.text: Solar Plexus Grounding. Place your hand firmly on your solar plexus (just below the ribs). Feel the warmth of your palm and say silently: 'I am here, and I am steady.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a tactile anchor for the diaphragm, encouraging a lower center of gravity and pulling the awareness back into the physical midline.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Charged
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel charged. You have 'plugged in' to a concern, and your system is providing the electricity needed to pay attention and solve it.
pathB.blueprint: Attentional Arousal. Your sympathetic system is providing a low-level 'spark' to keep your brain sharp and your body ready to act on a concern.
pathB.physicalSensation: A light 'buzzing' in the forehead; ears that feel extra sensitive to sound; and a subtle 'ready' posture.
pathB.metaphor: A battery that has just been placed on the charger; there is a steady flow of energy starting to build up.
pathB.theImpulse: To ask questions, to double-check details, and to stay 'on alert' until the concern is resolved.
pathB.superpower: Focused Vigilance. You are highly 'online' and capable of catching small errors or risks before they grow.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The 'charge' is making your thoughts race. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Input Reduction. Lower the 'voltage' of the room right now: dim the lights, silence your phone notifications, or move to a quieter corner. Limit the incoming data.
body.physioTip: Best for: Manually lowering the amount of sensory data the 'Charged' brain has to process, preventing a spike into higher sympathetic arousal.

mind.text: Visual Dilation. Find the largest neutral surface in the room (a blank wall or the ceiling). Let your eyes rest there for 20 seconds without 'looking' for anything specific.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Switching off the foveal 'target-lock' visual mode that fuels hyper-vigilance, allowing the visual system to settle by looking 'out' at nothing.

social.text: The Concern Share. Tell a safe person: 'I am feeling a bit charged about [X].' If alone, say it out loud to the room. Just naming the electricity helps it move.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'affect labeling' to move the concern from the amygdala to the language centers, externalizing the internal charge into the social field.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels full of 'potential' problems. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Heel-Strike Grounding. If possible, take off your shoes. Press your heels firmly into the floor as if you are trying to leave an imprint. Hold for 5 seconds and release.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-pressure input at the base of the body to pull awareness back down from the 'head-heavy' attentional buzz and into the somatic midline.

mind.text: Gravitational Loading. Close your eyes for a moment. Focus entirely on the weight of your pelvis and thighs in the chair. Feel the downward pull of gravity holding you.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'heavy' sensory data from the lower body to counteract the 'light/buzzy' feeling of head-centered anxiety, anchoring you back in.

social.text: The Priority Filter. Identify the most urgent concern. Set a mental timer to think only about that ONE thing. Breathe and feel that specific focus in your chest.
social.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the 'processing tax' by collapsing multiple external alerts into a single internal stream, allowing the system to inhabit one point of focus.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Unsettled
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel unsettled. Your system is in a 'wait and see' mode, sensing that things aren't quite aligned, which creates a mild internal friction.
pathB.blueprint: Low-grade Orienting Tension. Your nervous system is scanning for a missing piece of safety or information, keeping you in a state of 'unguarded' readiness.
pathB.physicalSensation: A 'shifting' feeling in the chest; a need to move your feet; and eyes that won't settle on one point for very long.
pathB.metaphor: Dust motes dancing in a beam of light—constantly moving, never quite landing, waiting for the air to go still.
pathB.theImpulse: To pace, to adjust your clothing, and to 'look around' for the source of the unease.
pathB.superpower: Intuitive Sensing. You are picking up on a subtle mismatch in your environment. Trust your system's ability to 'notice' the nuances.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'shiftiness'. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Tactile Discriminatory Scan. Reach out and touch 3 different textures in your immediate environment: something hard (desk), something soft (fabric), and something cold (metal). Identify the distinct difference in your mind.
body.physioTip: Best for: Shifting the brain from 'distal threat scanning' (eyes/ears) to 'proximal safety testing' (hands), pulling focus out of internal unease and into the physical room.

mind.text: The 20-Second Fixation. Pick a single, small, unmoving spot on the wall or a piece of furniture. Look at it steadily for 20 seconds without letting your eyes wander.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Manually inhibiting the superior colliculus's urge to 'scan,' proving to the brain that it is safe to remain still in this environment.

social.text: The Mundane Anchor. If with someone, ask: 'What’s on your schedule for tomorrow?' If alone, look at your own calendar. Focus entirely on practical, boring, external details.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing social entrainment to shift the brain away from abstract internal unease toward concrete, external reality.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels 'off'. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Ischial Tuberosity Grounding. Sit firmly on a hard chair. Rock slightly until you feel the sharp 'sit-bones' in your pelvis, then settle your full weight directly onto them. Feel the vertical support.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using skeletal loading to provide a clear 'vertical anchor' for a nervous system that feels internally untethered, pulling focus back into the physical midline.

mind.text: The 'Right Now' List. Name 3 things that are biologically true for your body in this exact second (e.g., 'My feet are touching the floor', 'I can feel the air in my nostrils').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Interrupting the 'future-threat' scanning by forcing the brain to process immediate, non-negotiable internal sensory data.

social.text: Bilateral Humerus Compression. Cross your arms and give your own opposite upper arms (the humerus bone) a firm, steady squeeze. Hold for 10 seconds and feel your own boundaries.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a 'boundary' signal to the brain, using self-touch to activate the ventral vagal system and pull the 'spaced out' awareness back into the skin.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Tense
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel tense. You’ve encountered a small 'knot' in your day, and your muscles are tightening to help you pull through it.
pathB.blueprint: Mild Sympathetic Tone. A slight increase in muscle spindles' sensitivity, preparing your body to exert force if the 'peeved' feeling escalates.
pathB.physicalSensation: A tight jaw; a 'string' pulling between the shoulder blades; and a feeling of being 'wound up' like a spring.
pathB.metaphor: A guitar string that has been tuned just a half-note too sharp. The sound is clear, but it feels like it might snap if pulled harder.
pathB.theImpulse: To sigh with frustration, to move with 'sharp' gestures, and to be short with your answers.
pathB.superpower: Ready-to-Act. This tension is 'work-ready' energy. It is the beginning of the focus needed to solve a problem or set a boundary.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the 'knot' and frustration internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Foveal Break. Look at the furthest point possible—out a window or down a long hallway. Soften your gaze until the edges of the room feel slightly blurry for 15 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Inhibiting narrow foveal vision to shift the brain from a 'threat-fixated' sympathetic state to a 'global-scanning' parasympathetic state, pulling awareness out of the internal knot.

mind.text: Relaxation Mirroring. Identify one object in your environment that is 'limp' or 'draped' (e.g., a scarf on a chair, a hanging plant leaf, a sleeping pet). Look at it and imagine having its weight.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using external visual 'anchors' of low-tone objects to prime the brain for its own muscular down-regulation by looking 'at' relaxation in the world.

social.text: Pharyngeal Reset. Perform a wide, exaggerated 'fake' yawn. Stretch the back of your throat and open your mouth as wide as possible, exhaling with a soft sound.
social.physioTip: Best for: Mechanically stretching the muscles of the jaw and pharynx to stimulate the glossopharyngeal nerve, triggering a reset that opens the internal 'clench' to the outside.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels annoying or high-pressure. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Scapular Drop. Inhale deeply while pulling your shoulders toward your ears. Hold for 2 seconds, then exhale sharply and let your shoulders drop like heavy weights.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using brief, high-intensity contraction followed by a gravity-assisted release to reset the tone of the upper trapezius, pulling focus back to the physical midline.

mind.text: The Micro-Softening. Close your eyes. Focus only on the corners of your mouth. Soften them by 10% and imagine the space between your upper and lower molars widening.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the facial feedback loop to send a 'low-threat' signal to the brainstem, reducing secondary jaw tension and anchoring you back in your skin.

social.text: The Sigh Signal. Text a 'sigh' emoji 😮‍💨 to a trusted friend or say 'sigh' out loud to yourself. Acknowledge your state without starting a complex conversation.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing minor irritation to prevent it from 'stewing', using a simple social anchor to reclaim your internal space from environmental annoyance.

pathB.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

pathB.title: Still
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel still. Your system has reached a rare point of total equilibrium where no effort is required. You are safely anchored in the present moment.
pathB.blueprint: Ventral Vagal dominance. Your parasympathetic system is in its most restorative 'rest and digest' mode. Heart rate and breathing are at their most efficient, rhythmic baseline.
pathB.physicalSensation: A feeling of weightlessness or 'soft' boundaries; muscles are completely devoid of holding-tension; breath is deep, slow, and effortless.
pathB.metaphor: A mountain lake at dawn—the water is so flat it acts as a perfect mirror, undisturbed by even a single ripple.
pathB.theImpulse: To remain exactly as you are, to breathe deeply, and to simply 'be' without any need to do.
pathB.superpower: Deep Restoration. In this state of total stillness, your body is performing its most vital cellular and emotional repair work.
pathB.generalGoal: Savoring the sanctuary. Deepening the rest.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are deeply submerged in internal peace. Goal: Quiet Awareness.

body.text: Neutral Point Scanning. Scan your body for the most 'neutral' or quiet spot (e.g., your earlobes, the back of your knees). Simply rest your attention there.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reinforcing the brain's map of 'safety-points' in the body, which serves as a physiological anchor for future high-stress states.

mind.text: Cloud Watching. Allow thoughts to appear and pass like distant clouds. Observe them without 'grabbing' or analyzing them.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Practicing 'metacognitive awareness'—shifting from being the thinker to the observer of thoughts, which reduces cognitive load.

social.text: The Luminous Perimeter. Visualize a soft circle of light surrounding you, acting as a gentle, permeable boundary that keeps this peace secure.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using symbolic imagery to maintain the Ventral Vagal state in shared spaces without needing to physically isolate.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels distant and non-threatening. Goal: Gentle Inclusion.

body.text: Atmospheric Boundary Sensing. Notice the subtle feeling of the air moving against your skin. Observe the temperature and the weight of your clothing.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing low-threshold mechanoreceptors to confirm environmental safety, further deepening the 'rest and digest' response.

mind.text: Harmonic Orienting. Identify one distant, repetitive sound (wind, a clock). Notice how your internal stillness effortlessly absorbs the sound.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-engaging the auditory system in a non-threatening way, preventing the 'startle reflex' from being triggered by sudden changes.

social.text: Environmental Gratitude. Mentally or whisper a 'thank you' to your current surroundings for providing this window of absolute safety.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using gratitude to stimulate the prefrontal cortex's 'reward' pathways, chemically cementing the state of serenity.

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Warm
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel warm. Your system has relaxed its 'constriction,' allowing blood and energy to flow freely to your skin and extremities. You are safe and held.
pathB.blueprint: Optimal Vasodilation. Your nervous system is signaling that the environment is hospitable, allowing the body to release its 'survival' grip and expand into a state of comfort.
pathB.physicalSensation: A pleasant 'glow' in the chest and belly; soft, warm hands and feet; and a feeling of 'melting' into your seat.
pathB.metaphor: Sunlight hitting a patch of moss in the forest; a slow, steady radiance that seeps into everything it touches.
pathB.theImpulse: To snuggle, to wrap yourself in something soft, and to let your eyes go heavy and soft.
pathB.superpower: Somatic Comfort. You are radiating safety. This warmth is the biological 'signal' that allows for deep social bonding and healing.
pathB.generalGoal: Nurturing the glow. Expanding the comfort.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a beautiful internal glow. Goal: Internal Radiance.

body.text: Thermal Biofeedback. Place your palms firmly on your belly or chest. Focus entirely on the heat transfer from your core to your hands.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing cutaneous thermoreceptors to confirm the body's 'expansion' state, reinforcing the Ventral Vagal safety signal.

mind.text: Golden Flow. Imagine a warm, golden liquid slowly filling your body from the soles of your feet to the crown of your head.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'visceral imagery' to facilitate systemic relaxation and further lower any residual sympathetic tone.

social.text: The Belonging Recall. Recall a specific moment when you felt completely 'at home' or safe with another person. Focus on the physical feeling of that memory.
social.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain's 'attachment circuitry' (Oxytocin/Vasopressin) to deepen the state of safe social engagement.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels inviting and soft. Goal: Tactile Savoring.

body.text: The Softness Scan. Find a soft texture (sweater, blanket, or skin) and move your fingertips over it at a rate of 3cm per second.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating C-tactile fibers—specialized nerves that only respond to slow, gentle touch and send 'pro-social' safety signals directly to the insula.

mind.text: Comfort Inventory. Identify 3 objects in the room that look 'comfortable' or 'hospitable'. Notice how your body responds to seeing them.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Training the brain to 'seek comfort' rather than 'scan for threats,' shifting the baseline of environmental appraisal.

social.text: The Compassionate Wrap. Imagine yourself wrapping a heavy, warm blanket around a person you care about. See them relax as you do.
social.physioTip: Best for: Moving from 'self-safety' to 'co-regulation,' which strengthens the Ventral Vagal branch and social connection.

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Quiet
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel quiet. Your internal 'noise' has settled, and your system is no longer scanning for problems. You are in a state of tranquil observation.
pathB.blueprint: Reduced Auditory Vigilance. Your nervous system has tuned out the 'high-frequency' scanning for danger, allowing you to hear the subtle, rhythmic sounds of life and safety.
pathB.physicalSensation: A lack of 'buzzing' in the head; relaxed ears; and a sense of space behind the forehead. The world feels 'muffled' in a pleasant, soft way.
pathB.metaphor: A library in the middle of a forest; the only sounds are the turning of pages and the rustle of leaves.
pathB.theImpulse: To whisper, to move slowly, and to listen to the silence between sounds.
pathB.superpower: Clarity of Mind. In this quiet, you can perceive the most subtle details and truths that are usually drowned out by the noise of survival.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a deep, hushed internal space. Goal: Inner Listening.

body.text: Interoceptive Audition. Close your eyes and focus entirely on the sound of your own heartbeat or the soft 'whoosh' of your breath in your throat.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the 'midline' of the body, utilizing the rhythm of the heart to provide a biological anchor for peace.

mind.text: The Gap Search. Notice the space or the 'gap' between your thoughts. Observe how long that silence lasts before the next thought appears.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Practicing 'non-attachment' to cognitive noise, which helps maintain low sympathetic arousal and mental clarity.

social.text: The Silent 'I Am'. Enjoy the quiet company of your own presence. Whisper or say silently: 'I am here, and it is quiet.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using self-affirmation to cement the sense of safety, reinforcing the Ventral Vagal state of social engagement with the self.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels peaceful and still. Goal: Audio Savoring.

body.text: The Floor-Noise Scan. Listen for the quietest, most constant sound you can find (a distant hum, a clock, or the air moving). Focus on its steadiness.
body.physioTip: Best for: Training the auditory system to rest on neutral stimuli, which lowers the threshold for 'startle' responses.

mind.text: Visual Silence. Pick a neutral-colored surface. Observe the 'quietness' of the color and the lack of movement on the texture.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing visual-motor activity to match the auditory quietness, creating a total-system state of tranquility.

social.text: The Silent Proxy. Look at a photo of a safe person. Imagine the 'quiet comfort' of their presence without needing to exchange words.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing symbolic social connection to maintain a high-safety vital (+3) without the metabolic cost of active communication.

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Grounded
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel grounded. Your system is fully supported by the environment, and you have 'dropped' your weight into that support.
pathB.blueprint: Optimal Proprioceptive Feedback. Your nervous system is receiving strong signals from your muscles and joints that you are stable, leading to a sense of physical 'heaviness' and emotional security.
pathB.physicalSensation: A strong awareness of your feet and seat; a feeling of being 'solid' and unshakeable; and a low center of gravity in the pelvis.
pathB.metaphor: An anchor at the bottom of a calm bay. There is no tension in the chain, and the sand holds the weight perfectly.
pathB.theImpulse: To exhale deeply, to sink further into your seat, and to enjoy the feeling of 'not having to go anywhere'.
pathB.superpower: Unshakeable Stability. When you are grounded, you become a 'safe harbor' for others. You are the 'floor' that people can stand on.
pathB.generalGoal: Deepening the yield. Transitioning from 'holding' to 'being held'.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (Heaviness)
pathA.goal: The 'weight' feels a bit too internal or sleepy. Goal: Upward Expansion.

body.text: The Pelvic Breath. As you inhale, imagine the breath traveling down to your sit-bones; as you exhale, feel your spine grow 1cm taller without losing the heavy seat.
body.physioTip: Best for: Preventing 'collapse' into lethargy by maintaining a micro-engagement of the deep postural stabilizers (multifidus) while remaining grounded.

mind.text: The Internal Compass. Keep your eyes closed. Identify exactly where North is based on your current seated position. Feel your orientation in space.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Gently re-engaging spatial awareness to prevent the 'internalized' heaviness from becoming a total withdrawal from the environment.

social.text: The Steady Beacon. Imagine yourself as a lighthouse. You are fixed and heavy at the base, but your awareness (the light) is scanning the horizon with ease.
social.physioTip: Best for: Transitioning from 'internal rest' to 'social readiness' by using imagery that combines stability with outward-facing awareness.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (Disconnection)
pathB.goal: You are 'hovering' over the support rather than sinking into it. Goal: The Deep Yield.

body.text: Passive Loading. Identify one body part (like your forearms or calves) and consciously 'give' its entire weight to the surface it's touching. Let the chair do 100% of the work.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reducing residual 'background' muscle tone (guarding) by proving to the brain that the environment is physically capable of supporting the load.

mind.text: Structural Solidarity. Find a vertical line (a wall or pillar). Look at it and think: 'That wall is holding the roof, and the floor is holding me. We are both supported.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using visual structural cues to provide the 'logic' for emotional safety, facilitating a deeper parasympathetic settle.

social.text: The Shared Floor. Notice the person nearest to you (or a pet). Realize you are both being held up by the same earth. Feel the silent, solid connection beneath you.
social.physioTip: Best for: Creating a sense of 'common humanity' and social safety by anchoring to a shared physical reality (the ground).

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Slowing
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are slowing down. Your system is down-shifting its gears, moving from 'doing' into 'recovering'. You are preparing for deep rest.
pathB.blueprint: Transition to Sleep/Rest. Your cortisol is dropping and your melatonin/adenosine are rising, signaling to your motor system that the day's work is done.
pathB.physicalSensation: Heavy eyelids; a slow, rhythmic pulse; a desire to yawn; and a gradual 'blurring' of the edges of your thoughts.
pathB.metaphor: A record player slowing down after the power is turned off; the music is still there, but the tempo is dropping into a deep, low hum.
pathB.theImpulse: To close your eyes, to lean your head back, and to let go of any remaining 'to-do' lists.
pathB.superpower: The Gift of Sleep. You are entering the most powerful recovery state known to biology. You are surrendering to the natural rhythm of life.
pathB.generalGoal: Surrendering to the rhythm. Dimming the lights.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the internal 'slowing' of your engine. Goal: Soft Surrender.
pathA.body: The Long Exhale. Inhale for 3, exhale through your mouth for 6. Let your body go 'limp' on the exhale.
pathA.mind: Imagine the day's events being put away in a box, one by one.
pathA.social: Give yourself permission to go 'offline'. Say silently: 'The day is done.'

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels like it's dimming. Goal: Sensory Softening.
pathB.body: Slowly blink your eyes. Notice the 'heavy' feeling of the lids.
pathB.mind: Identify one sound that is rhythmic and constant (a fan, your breath) and follow it into rest.
pathB.social: Imagine a safe, loving presence sitting by your side, keeping watch while you rest.

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Light
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel light. Your system has released its 'survival weight,' allowing you to feel buoyant and unburdened. You are experiencing the absence of gravity's emotional pull.
pathB.blueprint: Ventral Vagal ease. The 'Vagal Brake' is applied gently, just enough to keep you calm but leaving you with a sense of airy freedom and low muscular effort.
pathB.physicalSensation: A feeling of fluffiness in the chest; a lack of pressure in the joints; and the sensation that you are floating slightly above your seat.
pathB.metaphor: A dandelion seed drifting on a gentle breeze—completely supported by the air, with no specific destination required.
pathB.theImpulse: To hum, to move with fluid gestures, and to look at the world with a sense of 'playful un-attachment'.
pathB.superpower: Buoyancy. In this state, you can navigate life's small bumps without sinking. You are biologically resilient and flexible.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel wonderfully airy and light. Goal: Internal Expansion.
pathA.body: Reach your arms out to the sides and take a deep, 'wide' breath. Feel the space you take up.
pathA.mind: The 'Cloud Watch'. Imagine your thoughts are light as feathers, just drifting through a clear blue sky.
pathA.social: Smile at yourself in a mirror. Notice the lack of tension in your eyes and brow.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels bright and easy. Goal: Sensory Savoring.
pathB.body: Walk a few steps and notice the 'spring' in your step. Feel the light bounce of your heels.
pathB.mind: Look at the highest point you can see (the sky or a tall building) and savor the height.
pathB.social: Share a lighthearted thought or a joke with someone nearby.

pathB.warning: N/A

pathB.title: Settled
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel settled. You have found a 'landing spot' where your needs are met, and your system has given you the signal that it’s safe to stay a while.
pathB.blueprint: Consolidated Safety. Your heart rate is steady and your digestive system is active (rest-and-digest). Your brain has stopped looking for the 'next' thing and is content with 'this' thing.
pathB.physicalSensation: A pleasant heaviness in the hips; a 'full' feeling in the belly; and a deep, natural rhythm to your breathing.
pathB.metaphor: A cat curled up in a patch of sun—completely relaxed, yet fully aware of the warmth and comfort of the spot.
pathB.theImpulse: To lean back, to exhale a long 'Ahhhh,' and to let the environment hold your full weight.
pathB.superpower: Presence. Because you aren't trying to go anywhere else, you are fully available for whatever—or whoever—is right here.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a deep internal 'okayness'. Goal: Core Comfort.
pathA.body: Place your hands on your lap, palms down. Feel the solid weight of your hands on your legs.
pathA.mind: Say silently: 'I have arrived. There is nowhere else I need to be right now.'
pathA.social: Visualize your 'safe place'—a room or nature spot where you feel most settled.

pathB.social: Enjoy the 'shared quiet' if someone is with you. Notice how safe it feels to not talk.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a quiet internal strength. Goal: Vertical Alignment.
pathA.body: Imagine a plumb line hanging from the top of your head to your tailbone. Feel the balance.

pathA.social: Offer a steady, calm gaze to someone or something. Be the 'unmoving' observer.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a clear internal 'lens'. Goal: Mindful Observation.
pathA.body: Rest your hands on a surface and feel the temperature of the air moving over your skin.
pathA.mind: Ask yourself a meaningful question and wait for the 'steady' answer to emerge.
pathA.social: Visualize yourself handling a future challenge with this same steadiness.

pathB.title: Loose
pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel loose. Your muscles have received the 'all-clear' signal and have let go of their readiness to fight or flee. You are in a state of safe flexibility.
pathB.blueprint: Myofascial Release. Your nervous system has decreased the 'gamma bias' (the baseline tension) in your muscles, allowing your connective tissues to soften and lengthen.
pathB.physicalSensation: Shoulders that hang low; a soft, relaxed belly; and limbs that feel like 'cooked noodles' or swaying willow branches.
pathB.metaphor: A hammock swaying in a gentle breeze—it has no rigid structure of its own, but it is perfectly supported and flexible.
pathB.theImpulse: To stretch lazily, to lounge, and to move with a 'flow' that doesn't care about efficiency or speed.
pathB.superpower: Adaptability. Because you are loose, you can flow around obstacles rather than crashing into them. You are biologically 'fluid'.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel soft and pliable internally. Goal: Fluid Awareness.
pathA.body: Gently roll your wrists or ankles in slow circles. Feel the ease of the movement.
pathA.mind: Imagine your body is made of water, flowing into whatever shape you are currently sitting in.
pathA.social: Send a 'calm' or 'wave' emoji 🌊 to someone just to share the vibe.

pathB.blueprint: Ventral Vagal Coherence. Your sympathetic and parasympathetic systems are working in perfect harmony, providing a steady baseline of safety and availability.
pathB.physicalSensation: A sense of 'symmetry' in the body; weight distributed evenly across your feet or seat; and a calm, rhythmic pulse that feels effortlessly steady.

pathB.theImpulse: To move with grace, to listen deeply, and to approach tasks with a clear, non-reactive mind.
pathB.superpower: Emotional Equilibrium. In this state, you are the 'anchor' in any room. You can process information without being swayed by the 'winds' of stress or urgency.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are enjoying a perfect internal center. Goal: Mindful Alignment.

pathA.mind: Visualize a golden thread running perfectly straight through the center of your body.
pathA.social: Acknowledge your own stability. Say silently: 'I am my own center.'

pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel present. Your 'thinking brain' and 'feeling body' are in the same room at the same time. You aren't stuck in the past or racing toward the future.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a beautiful internal clarity. Goal: Sensory Integration.
pathA.body: Notice the exact point where your breath turns from an inhale into an exhale.
pathA.mind: Name one thing you are grateful for in this exact moment, no matter how small.
pathA.social: Mentally send a 'blessing' or a kind thought to someone you care about.

pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are restoring. Your system has recognized a safe window to perform 'preventative maintenance'. You are refilling your tank in real-time.
pathB.blueprint: Active Metabolic Recovery. Your body is prioritizing anabolism (building and repairing) over catabolism (breaking down energy for action). You are in 'Tune-Up' mode.
pathB.physicalSensation: A tingling or 'effervescent' feeling in the muscles; a soft, easy depth to your breath; and a sense of 'replenishment' in your mind.

pathB.superpower: Capacity Building. You aren't just resting; you are increasing your future resilience. You are 'widening your window' of tolerance.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a gentle internal 'repair' happening. Goal: Internal Nourishment.
pathA.body: Place your hands over your eyes for 30 seconds. Enjoy the 'recharge' of total darkness.
pathA.mind: Imagine a cool, healing light washing through any part of your body that feels tired.
pathA.social: Give yourself permission to be 'unproductive' for the next 10 minutes.

pathB.validation: It makes sense that you feel calm. Your 'threat detectors' have gone into standby mode, allowing your system to enjoy a state of quiet readiness.
pathB.blueprint: Parasympathetic Dominance. The Vagal Brake is firmly but comfortably applied. Your heart rate is low, and your nervous system is 'open' to the world.
